182	
183	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
184	# Store
185	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
186	
187	
188	class SupportTicketStore:
189	    """SQLite-backed support-ticket queue.
190	
191	    Per-call connections (matches the rest of the audit_ir
192	    pattern). WAL mode + foreign keys + BEGIN IMMEDIATE on
193	    transitions.
194	    """
195	
196	    def __init__(self, db_path: Path) -> None:
197	        self._db_path = Path(db_path)
198	        self._db_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
199	        self._init_schema()
200	
201	    def _connect(self) -> sqlite3.Connection:
202	        conn = sqlite3.connect(
203	            self._db_path, isolation_level=None, timeout=30.0,
204	        )
205	        conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
206	        conn.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL")
207	        conn.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON")
208	        return conn
209	
210	    def _init_schema(self) -> None:
211	        with self._connect() as conn:
212	            conn.executescript(_SCHEMA)
213	
214	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
215	    # Create
216	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
217	
218	    def open_ticket(
219	        self,
220	        *,
221	        org_id: str,
222	        submitter_user_id: str,
223	        title: str,
224	        description: str,
225	        category: TicketCategory,
226	        priority: TicketPriority = TicketPriority.NORMAL,
227	        related_run_slug: str | None = None,
228	        related_review_id: str | None = None,
229	        related_workspace_id: str | None = None,
230	    ) -> SupportTicket:
231	        """Open a new ticket. The body of `description` may grow
232	        via append_message later; this call is for the initial
233	        problem statement."""
234	        if not org_id.strip():
235	            raise SupportTicketStateError("org_id must be non-empty")
236	        if not submitter_user_id.strip():
237	            raise SupportTicketStateError(
238	                "submitter_user_id must be non-empty"
239	            )
240	        if not title.strip():
241	            raise SupportTicketStateError("title must be non-empty")
242	        if not description.strip():
243	            raise SupportTicketStateError(
244	                "description must be non-empty so support has "
245	                "context to triage"
246	            )
247	        if not isinstance(category, TicketCategory):
248	            raise SupportTicketStateError(
249	                f"category must be TicketCategory, got {category!r}"
250	            )
251	        if not isinstance(priority, TicketPriority):
252	            raise SupportTicketStateError(
253	                f"priority must be TicketPriority, got {priority!r}"
254	            )
255	        ticket_id = f"sup_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}"
256	        now = time.time()
257	        with self._connect() as conn:
258	            conn.execute(
259	                "INSERT INTO support_tickets (ticket_id, org_id, "
260	                "submitter_user_id, title, description, category, "
261	                "priority, status, related_run_slug, related_review_id, "
262	                "related_workspace_id, created_at, updated_at) VALUES "
263	                "(?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)",
264	                (
265	                    ticket_id, org_id.strip(), submitter_user_id.strip(),
266	                    title.strip(), description.strip(),
267	                    category.value, priority.value,
268	                    TicketStatus.OPEN.value,
269	                    related_run_slug, related_review_id,
270	                    related_workspace_id, now, now,
271	                ),
272	            )
273	        return SupportTicket(
274	            ticket_id=ticket_id, org_id=org_id.strip(),
275	            submitter_user_id=submitter_user_id.strip(),
276	            title=title.strip(), description=description.strip(),
277	            category=category, priority=priority,
278	            status=TicketStatus.OPEN, assigned_to=None,
279	            related_run_slug=related_run_slug,
280	            related_review_id=related_review_id,
281	            related_workspace_id=related_workspace_id,
282	            created_at=now, updated_at=now, resolved_at=None,
283	        )
284	
285	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
286	    # State transitions
287	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
288	
289	    def assign(
290	        self, *, ticket_id: str, org_id: str,
291	        agent_user_id: str,
292	    ) -> SupportTicket:
293	        """Assign a ticket to a support agent. Transitions OPEN →
294	        IN_PROGRESS. Already-IN_PROGRESS tickets can be reassigned
295	        to a different agent without re-transitioning."""
296	        return self._mutate(
297	            ticket_id=ticket_id, org_id=org_id,
298	            agent_user_id=agent_user_id,
299	            allowed_from=(
300	                TicketStatus.OPEN, TicketStatus.IN_PROGRESS,
301	            ),
302	            new_status=TicketStatus.IN_PROGRESS,
303	            assign_to=agent_user_id,
304	        )
305	
306	    def resolve(
307	        self, *, ticket_id: str, org_id: str,
308	        agent_user_id: str,
309	    ) -> SupportTicket:
310	        """Mark a ticket resolved. Terminal state.
311	
312	        Codex M-24 v1 review fix: resolve() requires IN_PROGRESS,
313	        NOT OPEN. v1 allowed OPEN -> RESOLVED which bypassed the
314	        assign() step and left `assigned_to=None`.
315	
316	        Codex M-24 v2 review fix: resolve() ALSO requires
317	        assigned_to to be non-None. Without this, the bypass
318	        OPEN -> close() -> reopen() -> resolve() still produced
319	        a RESOLVED ticket with assigned_to=None (because reopen()
320	        doesn't re-assign and resolve() only checked status).
321	        v3 makes resolve() self-validate the assignee invariant.
322	        Operators wanting to close-without-assigning use close()
323	        instead, which permits OPEN as a from-state for won't-fix /
324	        duplicate scenarios.
325	        """
326	        return self._mutate(
327	            ticket_id=ticket_id, org_id=org_id,
328	            agent_user_id=agent_user_id,
329	            allowed_from=(TicketStatus.IN_PROGRESS,),
330	            new_status=TicketStatus.RESOLVED,
331	            mark_resolved=True,
332	            require_assignee=True,
333	        )
334	
335	    def close(
336	        self, *, ticket_id: str, org_id: str,
337	        agent_user_id: str,
338	    ) -> SupportTicket:
339	        """Close a ticket. Either resolved-and-closed (final) or
340	        won't-fix / duplicate-style closures."""
341	        return self._mutate(
342	            ticket_id=ticket_id, org_id=org_id,
343	            agent_user_id=agent_user_id,
344	            allowed_from=(
345	                TicketStatus.OPEN, TicketStatus.IN_PROGRESS,
346	                TicketStatus.RESOLVED,
347	            ),
348	            new_status=TicketStatus.CLOSED,
349	            mark_resolved=True,
350	        )
351	
352	    def reopen(
353	        self, *, ticket_id: str, org_id: str,
354	        agent_user_id: str,
355	    ) -> SupportTicket:
356	        """Re-open a RESOLVED or CLOSED ticket. Transitions back
357	        to IN_PROGRESS so the agent can keep working it."""
358	        return self._mutate(
359	            ticket_id=ticket_id, org_id=org_id,
360	            agent_user_id=agent_user_id,
361	            allowed_from=(
362	                TicketStatus.RESOLVED, TicketStatus.CLOSED,
363	            ),
364	            new_status=TicketStatus.IN_PROGRESS,
365	            clear_resolved=True,
366	        )
367	
368	    def _mutate(
369	        self,
370	        *,
371	        ticket_id: str,
372	        org_id: str,
373	        agent_user_id: str,
374	        allowed_from: tuple[TicketStatus, ...],
375	        new_status: TicketStatus,
376	        assign_to: str | None = None,
377	        mark_resolved: bool = False,
378	        clear_resolved: bool = False,
379	        require_assignee: bool = False,
380	    ) -> SupportTicket:
381	        if not agent_user_id.strip():
382	            raise SupportTicketStateError(
383	                "agent_user_id must be non-empty"
384	            )
385	        from_values = tuple(s.value for s in allowed_from)
386	        now = time.time()
387	        with self._connect() as conn:
388	            conn.execute("BEGIN IMMEDIATE")
389	            try:
390	                row = conn.execute(
391	                    "SELECT * FROM support_tickets WHERE ticket_id = ?",
392	                    (ticket_id,),
393	                ).fetchone()
394	                if row is None:
395	                    raise SupportTicketStateError(
396	                        f"ticket {ticket_id!r} not found"
397	                    )
398	                if row["org_id"] != org_id:
399	                    # Same posture as review_store: don't pretend
400	                    # the ticket doesn't exist; the router converts
401	                    # this to 403.
402	                    raise SupportTicketStateError(
403	                        f"ticket {ticket_id!r} belongs to a "
404	                        f"different org"
405	                    )
406	                current = TicketStatus(row["status"])
407	                if current not in allowed_from:
408	                    raise SupportTicketStateError(
409	                        f"ticket {ticket_id!r} is in state "
410	                        f"{current.value!r}; expected one of "
411	                        f"{from_values} to transition to "
412	                        f"{new_status.value!r}"
413	                    )
414	                # Codex M-24 v2 review fix: resolve() must verify
415	                # the ticket has an assignee. Catches the
416	                # OPEN -> close() -> reopen() -> resolve() backdoor
417	                # where reopen() restores IN_PROGRESS without
418	                # re-assigning.
419	                if require_assignee and not row["assigned_to"]:
420	                    raise SupportTicketStateError(
421	                        f"ticket {ticket_id!r} has no assignee; "
422	                        f"cannot transition to {new_status.value!r} "
423	                        f"without an assignee — call assign() first"
424	                    )
425	
426	                set_parts = ["status = ?", "updated_at = ?"]
427	                params: list[Any] = [new_status.value, now]
428	                if assign_to is not None:
429	                    set_parts.append("assigned_to = ?")
430	                    params.append(assign_to.strip())
431	                if mark_resolved:
432	                    set_parts.append("resolved_at = ?")
433	                    params.append(now)
434	                if clear_resolved:
435	                    set_parts.append("resolved_at = NULL")
436	                params.append(ticket_id)
437	                conn.execute(
438	                    f"UPDATE support_tickets SET {', '.join(set_parts)} "
439	                    f"WHERE ticket_id = ?",
440	                    params,
441	                )
442	                conn.execute("COMMIT")
443	            except Exception:
444	                conn.execute("ROLLBACK")
445	                raise
446	
447	        ticket = self.get_ticket(ticket_id=ticket_id, org_id=org_id)
448	        if ticket is None:  # defensive
449	            raise SupportTicketError(
450	                f"transitioned ticket {ticket_id!r} but cannot read it back"
451	            )
452	        return ticket
453	
454	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
455	    # Messages (append-only thread)
456	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
457	
458	    def append_message(
459	        self,
460	        *,
461	        ticket_id: str,
462	        org_id: str,
463	        author_user_id: str,
464	        body: str,
465	    ) -> SupportMessage:
466	        """Append a message to the ticket thread. Cross-org
467	        access raises so a foreign tenant cannot inject messages
468	        into another org's tickets."""
469	        if not author_user_id.strip():
470	            raise SupportTicketStateError(
471	                "author_user_id must be non-empty"
472	            )
473	        if not body.strip():
474	            raise SupportTicketStateError(
475	                "body must be non-empty"
476	            )
477	        # Verify the ticket exists in this org first.
478	        ticket = self.get_ticket(ticket_id=ticket_id, org_id=org_id)
479	        if ticket is None:
480	            raise SupportTicketStateError(
481	                f"ticket {ticket_id!r} is not accessible to this caller"
482	            )
483	        message_id = f"msg_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}"
484	        now = time.time()
485	        with self._connect() as conn:
486	            conn.execute(
487	                "INSERT INTO support_messages (message_id, "
488	                "ticket_id, author_user_id, body, created_at) "
489	                "VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)",
490	                (
491	                    message_id, ticket_id, author_user_id.strip(),
492	                    body.strip(), now,
493	                ),
494	            )
495	            # Bump the ticket's updated_at so the queue surfaces
496	            # the most-recently-active tickets first.
497	            conn.execute(
498	                "UPDATE support_tickets SET updated_at = ? "
499	                "WHERE ticket_id = ?",
500	                (now, ticket_id),
501	            )
502	        return SupportMessage(
503	            message_id=message_id, ticket_id=ticket_id,
504	            author_user_id=author_user_id.strip(),
505	            body=body.strip(), created_at=now,
506	        )
507	
508	    def list_messages(
509	        self, *, ticket_id: str, org_id: str,
510	    ) -> list[SupportMessage]:
511	        """Read the full thread for a ticket, oldest-first. Empty
512	        list if the ticket doesn't belong to org_id (no existence
513	        leak — same as review_store.list_transitions)."""
514	        ticket = self.get_ticket(ticket_id=ticket_id, org_id=org_id)
515	        if ticket is None:
516	            return []
517	        with self._connect() as conn:
518	            rows = conn.execute(
519	                "SELECT * FROM support_messages WHERE ticket_id = ? "
520	                "ORDER BY created_at ASC",
521	                (ticket_id,),
522	            ).fetchall()
523	        return [
524	            SupportMessage(
525	                message_id=r["message_id"],
526	                ticket_id=r["ticket_id"],
527	                author_user_id=r["author_user_id"],
528	                body=r["body"],
529	                created_at=float(r["created_at"]),
530	            )
531	            for r in rows
532	        ]
533	
534	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
535	    # Read paths
536	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
537	
538	    def get_ticket(
539	        self, *, ticket_id: str, org_id: str,
540	    ) -> SupportTicket | None:
541	        with self._connect() as conn:
542	            row = conn.execute(
543	                "SELECT * FROM support_tickets "
544	                "WHERE ticket_id = ? AND org_id = ?",
545	                (ticket_id, org_id),
546	            ).fetchone()
547	        if row is None:
548	            return None
549	        return _row_to_ticket(row)
550	
551	    def list_by_org(
552	        self,
553	        *,
554	        org_id: str,
555	        status: TicketStatus | None = None,
556	        category: TicketCategory | None = None,
557	        assigned_to: str | None = None,
558	    ) -> list[SupportTicket]:
559	        clauses = ["org_id = ?"]
560	        params: list[Any] = [org_id]
561	        if status is not None:
562	            clauses.append("status = ?")
563	            params.append(status.value)
564	        if category is not None:
565	            clauses.append("category = ?")
566	            params.append(category.value)
567	        if assigned_to is not None:
568	            clauses.append("assigned_to = ?")
569	            params.append(assigned_to)
570	        sql = (
571	            f"SELECT * FROM support_tickets WHERE "
572	            f"{' AND '.join(clauses)} ORDER BY updated_at DESC"
573	        )
574	        with self._connect() as conn:
575	            rows = conn.execute(sql, params).fetchall()
576	        return [_row_to_ticket(r) for r in rows]
577	
578	
579	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
580	# Serialization
581	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
582	
583	
584	def ticket_to_dict(t: SupportTicket) -> dict[str, Any]:
585	    return {
586	        "ticket_id": t.ticket_id,
587	        "org_id": t.org_id,
588	        "submitter_user_id": t.submitter_user_id,
589	        "title": t.title,
590	        "description": t.description,
591	        "category": t.category.value,
592	        "priority": t.priority.value,
593	        "status": t.status.value,
594	        "assigned_to": t.assigned_to,
595	        "related_run_slug": t.related_run_slug,
596	        "related_review_id": t.related_review_id,
597	        "related_workspace_id": t.related_workspace_id,
598	        "created_at": t.created_at,
599	        "updated_at": t.updated_at,
600	        "resolved_at": t.resolved_at,
601	    }
602	
603	
604	def message_to_dict(m: SupportMessage) -> dict[str, Any]:
605	    return {
606	        "message_id": m.message_id,
607	        "ticket_id": m.ticket_id,
608	        "author_user_id": m.author_user_id,
609	        "body": m.body,
610	        "created_at": m.created_at,
611	    }
612	
613	
614	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
615	# Row → object converter
616	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
617	
618	
619	def _row_to_ticket(row: sqlite3.Row) -> SupportTicket:
620	    return SupportTicket(
621	        ticket_id=row["ticket_id"],
622	        org_id=row["org_id"],
623	        submitter_user_id=row["submitter_user_id"],
624	        title=row["title"],
625	        description=row["description"],
626	        category=TicketCategory(row["category"]),
627	        priority=TicketPriority(row["priority"]),
628	        status=TicketStatus(row["status"]),
629	        assigned_to=row["assigned_to"],
630	        related_run_slug=row["related_run_slug"],
631	        related_review_id=row["related_review_id"],
632	        related_workspace_id=row["related_workspace_id"],
633	        created_at=float(row["created_at"]),
634	        updated_at=float(row["updated_at"]),
635	        resolved_at=(
636	            float(row["resolved_at"]) if row["resolved_at"] is not None
637	            else None
638	        ),
639	    )

1	"""Curated template catalog (M-10 — Phase B foundation).
2	
3	Per FINAL_PLAN.md, the curated template router is the Phase B
4	mitigation for Risk #13 (query-to-template misrouting / unsupported-
5	query overclaim). Phase B ships with a SINGLE working template
6	(v30_clinical) plus the routing infrastructure; new templates are
7	added to this catalog as data, no router-code change required.
8	
9	The catalog also doubles as the data source for the "supported scope"
10	page (FINAL_PLAN scope-page reinforcement mitigation): UI surfaces
11	each entry's display_name, description, and scope_summary so users
12	understand the bounds before submitting a query.
13	"""
14	
15	from __future__ import annotations
16	
17	from dataclasses import dataclass
18	
19	
20	@dataclass(frozen=True)
21	class CuratedTemplate:
22	    """A supported audit template with metadata for routing + scope display.
23	
24	    Codex M-10 review fix: signals are split into TWO classes to close
25	    the false-positive bypass identified in v1:
26	
27	      drug_keywords   → STRONG signals (specific drug names + drug
28	                        classes). Required for the ROUTED verdict —
29	                        no drug-keyword hit means the verdict cannot
30	                        rise above OPERATOR_REVIEW regardless of
31	                        exemplar similarity. This is the Risk #13
32	                        guardrail: a query about supplements,
33	                        psychotherapy, or non-pharmaceutical
34	                        interventions cannot accidentally route to
35	                        v30_clinical just because it shares the
36	                        question scaffold of an exemplar.
37	
38	      medical_keywords → BROAD signals (regulatory bodies, trial
39	                        methodology terms, conditions, generic
40	                        outcomes, broad medical-domain words).
41	                        Indicates the query is plausibly medical
42	                        and merits OPERATOR_REVIEW, but never alone
43	                        sufficient for ROUTED.
44	
45	    `scope_keywords` is a property returning the union — kept for
46	    backwards compat with code that just wants the full bag.
47	
48	    Attributes:
49	        template_id: Stable identifier matching a registered JobRunner.
50	        display_name: Human-readable name shown on the scope page.
51	        description: One- to two-sentence description.
52	        scope_summary: Longer scope description; documents IN-scope
53	                       AND OUT-of-scope per FINAL_PLAN scope-page
54	                       reinforcement mitigation.
55	        drug_keywords: Tuple of specific drugs / drug classes. Multi-
56	                       word entries match as token sets.
57	        medical_keywords: Tuple of broad medical/clinical-trial/
58	                          regulatory/condition terms.
59	        scope_examples: Concrete real-shape positive query exemplars.
60	    """
61	
62	    template_id: str
63	    display_name: str
64	    description: str
65	    scope_summary: str
66	    drug_keywords: tuple[str, ...]
67	    medical_keywords: tuple[str, ...]
68	    scope_examples: tuple[str, ...]
69	
70	    @property
71	    def scope_keywords(self) -> tuple[str, ...]:
72	        """Backward-compat: union of drug + medical keywords."""
73	        return tuple(self.drug_keywords) + tuple(self.medical_keywords)
74	
75

77	# Phase B initial catalog
78	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

1	"""Curated-template scope-guardrail classifier (M-10 — Phase B).
2	
3	NOT a multi-template intent classifier. With one production template
4	(v30_clinical) the routing problem in Phase B is "is this query in
5	scope for v30_clinical: yes/maybe/no" — the FINAL_PLAN Risk #13
6	mitigation. Misframing matters: an over-eager classifier silently
7	routes off-scope queries to v30_clinical and the user gets a
8	polished-but-misframed audit. The defaults bias toward
9	`unsupported_scope` when uncertain.
10	
11	Verdicts:
12	  routed                    → high-confidence in scope; UI can offer to
13	                              auto-enqueue. Score >= floor_high.
14	  operator_review_required  → medium-confidence; UI surfaces the
15	                              candidate template to a human for
16	                              confirmation before enqueue. Score in
17	                              [floor_review, floor_high).
18	  unsupported_scope         → low-confidence; UI tells the user the
19	                              question isn't yet supported and points
20	                              them at the scope page. Score <
21	                              floor_review.
22	
23	The classifier is **advisory only**. The /api/inspector/jobs enqueue
24	endpoint still requires an explicit template_id. UI flow: call
25	/route → surface verdict → user confirms → call /jobs.
26	
27	Scoring (deliberately simple for Phase B):
28	  - Tokenize the query (lowercase, ascii-ish word/digit tokens).
29	  - For each catalog template:
30	      keyword_match: count of scope_keywords whose token-set is a
31	                     subset of the query tokens (multi-word entries
32	                     match as token sets).
33	      example_jaccard: max Jaccard similarity (intersection / union)
34	                       between query tokens and any scope_example's
35	                       tokens.
36	      score: discrete cascade — high keyword-AND-example overlap
37	             → routed; some-but-not-strong → operator_review;
38	             nothing → unsupported.
39	
40	Determinism: same query produces the same verdict on every call.
41	No randomness, no model state, no external calls.
42	"""
43	
44	from __future__ import annotations
45	
46	import os
47	import re
48	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
49	from enum import Enum
50	
51	from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.template_catalog import (
52	    CuratedTemplate,
53	    list_catalog,
54	)
55	
56

58	# Threshold defaults (env-overridable, per LAW VI)
59	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
60	
61	DEFAULT_FLOOR_HIGH = 0.55
62	DEFAULT_FLOOR_REVIEW = 0.30
63	# Codex M-20 phase-c: minimum score gap between top-1 and top-2
64	# template candidates for top-1 to win routing. If the gap is
65	# narrower than this, the verdict demotes to OPERATOR_REVIEW so
66	# the operator picks. With 50+ templates, a query that matches
67	# multiple drug-anchored templates needs human disambiguation.
68	DEFAULT_TIE_MARGIN = 0.10
69

72	    """Three-tier routing outcome — see module docstring."""
73	
74	    ROUTED = "routed"

76	    UNSUPPORTED = "unsupported_scope"

86	    single opaque keyword count. `keyword_hits` retained as the union
87	    for backwards compat with v1 consumers.
88	    """

109	@dataclass(frozen=True)
110	class RouterConfig:
111	    """Tunable thresholds for the classifier.
112	
113	    Defaults are conservative-high in the false-positive direction
114	    (it is better to surface a supported question as
115	    operator_review_required than to silently route an off-scope
116	    question to v30_clinical and produce a misframed audit).
117	
118	    Override via env:
119	      PG_TEMPLATE_ROUTER_FLOOR_HIGH    (default 0.55)
120	      PG_TEMPLATE_ROUTER_FLOOR_REVIEW  (default 0.30)
121	      PG_TEMPLATE_ROUTER_TIE_MARGIN    (default 0.10)
122	    """
123	
124	    floor_high: float = DEFAULT_FLOOR_HIGH
125	    floor_review: float = DEFAULT_FLOOR_REVIEW
126	    tie_margin: float = DEFAULT_TIE_MARGIN
127	
128	    @classmethod
129	    def from_env(cls) -> "RouterConfig":
130	        try:
131	            high = float(os.environ.get("PG_TEMPLATE_ROUTER_FLOOR_HIGH", DEFAULT_FLOOR_HIGH))
132	        except ValueError:
133	            high = DEFAULT_FLOOR_HIGH
134	        try:
135	            review = float(os.environ.get("PG_TEMPLATE_ROUTER_FLOOR_REVIEW", DEFAULT_FLOOR_REVIEW))
136	        except ValueError:
137	            review = DEFAULT_FLOOR_REVIEW
138	        try:
139	            tie_margin = float(os.environ.get(
140	                "PG_TEMPLATE_ROUTER_TIE_MARGIN", DEFAULT_TIE_MARGIN,
141	            ))
142	        except ValueError:
143	            tie_margin = DEFAULT_TIE_MARGIN
144	        high = max(0.0, min(1.0, high))
145	        review = max(0.0, min(high, review))
146	        tie_margin = max(0.0, min(1.0, tie_margin))
147	        return cls(
148	            floor_high=high, floor_review=review, tie_margin=tie_margin,
149	        )

152	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
153	# Tokenization + matching primitives
154	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
155	
156	# Codex M-10 v6 review fix: tokenizer now splits on hyphens.
157	# Previously the regex preserved hyphens within tokens so "glp-1"
158	# stayed one token. That caused the v6 partial: queries with
159	# common compound-modifier orthography ("phase-3", "type-2",
160	# "chronic-kidney") tokenized as single tokens ("phase-3" etc.)
161	# which didn't match the space-tokenized multi-word keyword
162	# "phase 3" via contiguous-subseq.
163	#
164	# Splitting on hyphens changes "glp-1" → ["glp", "1"] which is
165	# fine: the keyword "glp-1" splits the same way and matches as a
166	# multi-word contiguous subseq. Same for "sglt-2", "dpp-4",
167	# "double-blind", "meta-analysis", "long-term", etc. The Unicode
168	# hyphen translate above still runs first so all hyphen variants
169	# normalize before the split.
170	_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"[a-z0-9]+")
171	
172	# Codex M-10 review fix: normalize Unicode hyphens (non-breaking
173	# hyphen U+2011, en-dash U+2013, em-dash U+2014, minus sign U+2212,
174	# figure dash U+2012) to ASCII so copy-pasted text like "GLP‑1"
175	# tokenizes the same way as "GLP-1". Done at normalize-time, not
176	# inside the regex, so the regex stays simple/auditable.
177	_HYPHEN_TRANSLATE = str.maketrans({
178	    "‐": "-", "‑": "-", "‒": "-",
179	    "–": "-", "—": "-", "―": "-",
180	    "−": "-",
181	})
182	
183	# Codex M-10 review fix: stopword/scaffold suppression. Without this,
184	# a query that mimics an exemplar's question scaffold ("What is the
185	# efficacy of X for Y?") gets a high Jaccard from {what, is, the,
186	# of, for} alone, which let off-scope queries like "What is the
187	# efficacy of turmeric for arthritis?" auto-route. The suppression
188	# is small and conservative — it only removes question scaffold and
189	# core English function words, never medical content.
190	_STOPWORDS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
191	    "a", "an", "the",
192	    "and", "or", "but",
193	    "of", "in", "on", "at", "to", "for", "from", "with", "by",
194	    "is", "are", "was", "were", "be", "been", "being",
195	    "what", "which", "who", "whom", "why", "how", "when", "where",
196	    "do", "does", "did", "has", "have", "had",
197	    "this", "that", "these", "those",
198	    "as", "if", "than", "then", "so", "such",
199	    "i", "you", "we", "they", "he", "she", "it",
200	    "my", "your", "our", "their", "his", "her", "its",
201	    "new", "any", "some", "all", "both", "each", "every",
202	    # Codex M-10 v4 review fix: clinical-context filler verbs that
203	    # don't carry routing signal. Adding them avoids alien-tagging
204	    # legitimate queries like "Adolescent semaglutide use for
205	    # obesity" (now alien=0 instead of alien={use}).
206	    # NOT added: words like "alongside", "wherever", "whenever" —
207	    # those are nonsense-tail bypass attempts and should remain
208	    # alien so the gate catches them.
209	    "use", "used", "using", "uses",
210	    "given", "taking", "received", "receiving",
211	})
212	
213	
214	# Codex M-10 v7 review fix: normalize multi-character Roman
215	# numerals to Arabic digits so clinical-literature phrasings like
216	# "phase III" / "type II diabetes" match the catalog's "phase 3" /
217	# "type 2 diabetes" multi-word keywords.
218	#
219	# Single-character Romans (i/v/x) deliberately excluded — "i" is
220	# the English pronoun (already a stopword), "v" / "x" are too
221	# ambiguous (could be variables, volume X, etc.). "phase v" /
222	# "type x" are rare enough that operator_review fallback is fine.
223	_ROMAN_TO_ARABIC: dict[str, str] = {
224	    "ii": "2", "iii": "3", "iv": "4",
225	    "vi": "6", "vii": "7", "viii": "8",
226	    "ix": "9",
227	}
228	
229	
230	# Codex M-10 v7 review fix: hyphen-less drug-class abbreviations
231	# (GLP1, SGLT2, DPP4) get split so they tokenize identically to
232	# their hyphenated form (GLP-1 → [glp, 1] after the v6 hyphen
233	# split). Without this, "GLP1 agonists" and "GLP-1 agonists"
234	# tokenize differently and the multi-word kw "glp-1" matches
235	# the latter but not the former.
236	_COMPACT_DRUG_CLASS_SPLIT: dict[str, list[str]] = {
237	    "glp1": ["glp", "1"],
238	    "sglt2": ["sglt", "2"],
239	    "dpp4": ["dpp", "4"],
240	}
241	
242	
243	def _tokenize_raw_seq(text: str) -> list[str]:
244	    """Lowercased, hyphen-split, Roman/compact-normalized word/digit
245	    tokens as an ORDERED list.
246	
247	    Pipeline:
248	      1. Unicode hyphen variants → ASCII hyphen.
249	      2. Lowercase.
250	      3. Regex `[a-z0-9]+` extracts alphanumeric tokens (hyphens
251	         become token boundaries — Codex M-10 v6 fix).
252	      4. Roman numerals II/III/IV/VI..IX → Arabic (Codex M-10 v7 fix).
253	      5. Compact drug-class forms (GLP1 / SGLT2 / DPP4) → their
254	         hyphen-split equivalents (Codex M-10 v7 fix).
255	      6. Drop 1-char non-digit tokens to suppress noise.
256	    """
257	    if not text:
258	        return []
259	    normalized = text.translate(_HYPHEN_TRANSLATE).lower()
260	    raw = _TOKEN_RE.findall(normalized)
261	    out: list[str] = []
262	    for t in raw:
263	        if t in _ROMAN_TO_ARABIC:
264	            t = _ROMAN_TO_ARABIC[t]
265	        if t in _COMPACT_DRUG_CLASS_SPLIT:
266	            for part in _COMPACT_DRUG_CLASS_SPLIT[t]:
267	                if len(part) > 1 or part.isdigit():
268	                    out.append(part)
269	            continue
270	        if len(t) > 1 or t.isdigit():
271	            out.append(t)
272	    return out
273	
274	
275	def _tokenize_raw(text: str) -> frozenset[str]:
276	    """Frozenset variant — used where order doesn't matter
277	    (single-word lookups, alien-token computation, Jaccard)."""
278	    return frozenset(_tokenize_raw_seq(text))
279	
280	
281	def _filter_stopwords(tokens: frozenset[str]) -> frozenset[str]:
282	    """Drop scaffold words so they cannot inflate Jaccard similarity."""
283	    return frozenset(t for t in tokens if t not in _STOPWORDS)
284	
285	
286	def _contains_subseq(haystack: list[str], needle: list[str]) -> bool:
287	    """Return True if `needle` appears as a CONTIGUOUS subsequence
288	    in `haystack`. Codex M-10 v5 review fix: replaces set-membership
289	    for multi-word keyword matching."""
290	    n = len(needle)
291	    if n == 0 or n > len(haystack):
292	        return False
293	    for i in range(len(haystack) - n + 1):
294	        if haystack[i:i + n] == needle:
295	            return True
296	    return False
297	
298	
299	def _keyword_hits(
300	    qtokens_set: frozenset[str],
301	    qtokens_seq: list[str],
302	    keywords: tuple[str, ...],
303	) -> tuple[str, ...]:
304	    """Return the keywords whose tokens appear in the query.
305	
306	    Codex M-10 v5 review fix: matching depends on keyword arity:
307	      - 1-token keyword: set membership (any occurrence in query).
308	      - N-token keyword (N > 1): CONTIGUOUS subsequence in the
309	        ordered query token list. This prevents the cross-hit
310	        bypass where a query with "phase 3" + "type 2 diabetes"
311	        had set tokens {phase, 2} both present, falsely matching
312	        the multi-word keyword "phase 2".
313	    """
314	    hits: list[str] = []
315	    for kw in keywords:
316	        kw_seq = _tokenize_raw_seq(kw)
317	        if not kw_seq:
318	            continue
319	        if len(kw_seq) == 1:
320	            if kw_seq[0] in qtokens_set:
321	                hits.append(kw)
322	        else:
323	            if _contains_subseq(qtokens_seq, kw_seq):
324	                hits.append(kw)
325	    return tuple(hits)
326	
327	
328	def _max_example_jaccard(
329	    qtokens_filtered: frozenset[str], examples: tuple[str, ...]
330	) -> float:
331	    """Highest Jaccard similarity between the query and any exemplar.
332	
333	    Both sides are stopword-filtered (Codex M-10 fix). Without the
334	    filter, a query+exemplar that share `{what, is, the, of, for}`
335	    gets a misleading 0.5+ baseline from scaffold alone.
336	    """
337	    best = 0.0
338	    if not qtokens_filtered:
339	        return 0.0
340	    for ex in examples:
341	        ex_toks = _filter_stopwords(_tokenize_raw(ex))
342	        if not ex_toks:
343	            continue
344	        inter = qtokens_filtered & ex_toks
345	        union = qtokens_filtered | ex_toks
346	        if not union:
347	            continue
348	        j = len(inter) / len(union)
349	        if j > best:
350	            best = j
351	    return best
352	
353	
354	def _alien_tokens(
355	    qtokens_filtered: frozenset[str],
356	    drug_hits: tuple[str, ...],
357	    medical_hits: tuple[str, ...],
358	) -> frozenset[str]:
359	    """Codex M-10 v3 review fix: tokens in the (stopword-filtered)
360	    query that are NOT covered by any matched keyword.
361	
362	    Used by the ROUTED gate to detect bypasses where a query has a
363	    real drug + exemplar-shape prefix but a nonsense suffix
364	    ("tirzepatide phase 3 trial outcomes for video game addiction").
365	    The exemplar Jaccard alone is too forgiving — adding 2-3 alien
366	    words still leaves the matched portion dominant. Counting
367	    unrecognized content tokens directly closes that hole.
368	    """
369	    matched: set[str] = set()
370	    for kw in drug_hits:
371	        matched |= _tokenize_raw(kw)
372	    for kw in medical_hits:
373	        matched |= _tokenize_raw(kw)
374	    return qtokens_filtered - matched
375	
376	
377	# Codex M-10 v4 review fix: ROUTED tightened to alien_count == 0.
378	# v3's single-alien slack still let 1-token nonsense tails route
379	# ("tirzepatide for diabetes printer" → routed 0.70). Phase B
380	# treats vocabulary gaps as a safe-failure direction: if a real
381	# clinical query uses a word the catalog doesn't know yet, it
382	# falls to OPERATOR_REVIEW and the operator confirms (or expands
383	# the catalog). The vocabulary in template_catalog.medical_keywords
384	# is curated to cover the common clinical-query content words so
385	# this gate is rarely reached for legitimate queries.
386	_ROUTED_MAX_ALIEN_TOKENS = 0
387	
388	
389	def _score_template(
390	    qtokens_set: frozenset[str],
391	    qtokens_seq: list[str],
392	    tmpl: CuratedTemplate,
393	) -> tuple[float, tuple[str, ...], tuple[str, ...], float]:
394	    """Codex M-10 v3 review: discrete-tier score using two-class
395	    keyword signals + alien-token gate.
396	
397	    Returns (score in [0, 1], drug_hits, medical_hits, example_jaccard).
398	
399	    The ROUTED gate (tier A) requires ALL THREE:
400	      (1) at least one drug_keyword hit (a specific regulated drug
401	          or narrow drug-class abbreviation is named), AND
402	      (2) example_jaccard ≥ 0.30 on stopword-filtered tokens (the
403	          query shape matches a known exemplar, not just the
404	          question scaffold), AND
405	      (3) at most _ROUTED_MAX_ALIEN_TOKENS unrecognized content
406	          tokens (the query doesn't introduce nonsense beyond what
407	          the catalog covers).
408	
409	    Each guard alone is bypass-able. Combined they pin the false-
410	    positive surface tightly. Phase B with one template biases the
411	    route decision strongly toward false-negatives (those land in
412	    OPERATOR_REVIEW, which the operator can reclassify).
413	    """
414	    drug_hits = _keyword_hits(qtokens_set, qtokens_seq, tmpl.drug_keywords)
415	    medical_hits = _keyword_hits(qtokens_set, qtokens_seq, tmpl.medical_keywords)
416	    qtokens_filtered = _filter_stopwords(qtokens_set)
417	    ex_jac = _max_example_jaccard(qtokens_filtered, tmpl.scope_examples)
418	    alien = _alien_tokens(qtokens_filtered, drug_hits, medical_hits)
419	    n_alien = len(alien)
420	
421	    n_drug = len(drug_hits)
422	    n_medical = len(medical_hits)
423	
424	    # Tier A — ROUTED: drug-anchored, semantically aligned, and
425	    # alien-bounded. Score blends jaccard so very-strong matches
426	    # land near 1.0 and weak-but-passing matches sit just above
427	    # floor_high.
428	    if n_drug >= 1 and ex_jac >= 0.30 and n_alien <= _ROUTED_MAX_ALIEN_TOKENS:
429	        score = 0.55 + 0.45 * ex_jac
430	        return min(score, 1.0), drug_hits, medical_hits, ex_jac
431	
432	    # Tier B — OPERATOR_REVIEW: drug named but the query shape is
433	    # off-pattern OR the query has too much unrecognized content.
434	    if n_drug >= 1:
435	        # Score sits in the review band; jaccard contributes a
436	        # small nudge so a borderline exemplar match isn't lost in
437	        # logs. Alien-heavy drug queries top out at the lower end
438	        # of the review band.
439	        base = 0.40 + 0.10 * ex_jac
440	        if n_alien > _ROUTED_MAX_ALIEN_TOKENS:
441	            base = min(base, 0.45)
442	        return base, drug_hits, medical_hits, ex_jac
443	
444	    # Tier C — OPERATOR_REVIEW: no drug named, but multiple medical
445	    # signals + decent jaccard. Could be a question that needs a
446	    # drug specified by the operator (e.g. "What is the efficacy of
447	    # this new GLP-1 agonist?" with the drug name elided).
448	    if n_medical >= 3 and ex_jac >= 0.20:
449	        return 0.40, drug_hits, medical_hits, ex_jac
450	
451	    # Tier D — OPERATOR_REVIEW (low): some medical signal but no
452	    # drug and weak shape match.
453	    if n_medical >= 2:
454	        return 0.35, drug_hits, medical_hits, ex_jac
455	    if n_medical >= 1:
456	        return 0.30, drug_hits, medical_hits, ex_jac
457	
458	    # Tier E — UNSUPPORTED: weak lexical overlap but no medical
459	    # framing at all.
460	    if ex_jac >= 0.20:
461	        return 0.20, drug_hits, medical_hits, ex_jac
462	
463	    return 0.0, drug_hits, medical_hits, ex_jac
464

470	
471	def classify_query(
472	    question: str, config: RouterConfig | None = None
473	) -> RoutingResult:
474	    """Map a user query to a routing verdict.
475	
476	    Empty / whitespace-only queries return UNSUPPORTED with a
477	    helpful rationale (rather than 400-ing) so the UI can surface
478	    the same scope-page CTA in every off-scope branch.
479	    """
480	    if config is None:
481	        config = RouterConfig.from_env()
482	
483	    if not question or not question.strip():
484	        return RoutingResult(
485	            verdict=RoutingVerdict.UNSUPPORTED,
486	            template_id=None,
487	            confidence=0.0,
488	            candidates=(),
489	            rationale=(
490	                "Empty query. Provide a clinical drug-condition question; "
491	                "see the scope page for examples of supported queries."
492	            ),
493	        )
494	
495	    qtokens_seq = _tokenize_raw_seq(question)
496	    qtokens_set = frozenset(qtokens_seq)
497	    catalog = list_catalog()
498	    if not catalog:
499	        return RoutingResult(
500	            verdict=RoutingVerdict.UNSUPPORTED,
501	            template_id=None,
502	            confidence=0.0,
503	            candidates=(),
504	            rationale="No curated templates registered.",
505	        )
506	
507	    scored: list[RoutingCandidate] = []
508	    for tmpl in catalog:
509	        score, drug_hits, medical_hits, ex_jac = _score_template(
510	            qtokens_set, qtokens_seq, tmpl
511	        )
512	        scored.append(
513	            RoutingCandidate(
514	                template_id=tmpl.template_id,
515	                score=score,
516	                keyword_hits=drug_hits + medical_hits,
517	                drug_hits=drug_hits,
518	                medical_hits=medical_hits,
519	                example_jaccard=ex_jac,
520	            )
521	        )
522	    scored.sort(key=lambda c: c.score, reverse=True)
523	    candidates = tuple(scored)
524	    top = candidates[0]
525	
526	    if top.score >= config.floor_high:
527	        # Codex M-20 phase-c: intra-scope tie detection. With 50+
528	        # templates, multiple drug-anchored templates may score
529	        # within tie_margin of each other (e.g. tirzepatide for
530	        # diabetes vs tirzepatide for obesity). Demote to
531	        # OPERATOR_REVIEW when top-1 and top-2 are too close —
532	        # the operator picks which template matches the user's
533	        # actual question.
534	        if len(candidates) >= 2:
535	            top2 = candidates[1]
536	            gap = top.score - top2.score
537	            # Only fire the tie demotion if BOTH candidates are
538	            # over floor_high. If top-2 is below floor_high, the
539	            # margin doesn't matter — only top-1 is a viable
540	            # routing target.
541	            if (top2.score >= config.floor_high
542	                    and gap < config.tie_margin):
543	                return RoutingResult(
544	                    verdict=RoutingVerdict.OPERATOR_REVIEW,
545	                    template_id=top.template_id,
546	                    confidence=top.score,
547	                    candidates=candidates,
548	                    rationale=(
549	                        f"Multiple templates score above floor_high "
550	                        f"with a narrow gap: '{top.template_id}' "
551	                        f"({top.score:.2f}) vs '{top2.template_id}' "
552	                        f"({top2.score:.2f}); gap {gap:.2f} < "
553	                        f"tie_margin {config.tie_margin:.2f}. "
554	                        f"Operator must pick the right template."
555	                    ),
556	                )
557	        return RoutingResult(
558	            verdict=RoutingVerdict.ROUTED,
559	            template_id=top.template_id,
560	            confidence=top.score,
561	            candidates=candidates,
562	            rationale=(
563	                f"Query matches '{top.template_id}' with high confidence "
564	                f"(score {top.score:.2f} ≥ floor_high {config.floor_high:.2f}; "
565	                f"drug hits: {len(top.drug_hits)}, medical hits: "
566	                f"{len(top.medical_hits)}, example Jaccard: "
567	                f"{top.example_jaccard:.2f})."
568	            ),
569	        )
570	
571	    if top.score >= config.floor_review:
572	        # Codex M-10 review fix: rationale calls out which gate the
573	        # query failed so the operator-review UI can show "no
574	        # specific drug named — confirm or supply one" vs "drug
575	        # named but query shape is off" vs "query has unrecognized
576	        # content not covered by the catalog".
577	        # Codex M-10 v3: alien-token signal added.
578	        top_drug_hits_set = set(top.drug_hits)
579	        top_med_hits_set = set(top.medical_hits)
580	        # Alien is approximate here (full computation lives in
581	        # _score_template); we recompute a coarse version for the
582	        # rationale message.
583	        qfiltered = _filter_stopwords(_tokenize_raw(question))
584	        matched_set: set[str] = set()
585	        for kw in top_drug_hits_set | top_med_hits_set:
586	            matched_set |= _tokenize_raw(kw)
587	        alien_count = len(qfiltered - matched_set)
588	
589	        if not top.drug_hits:
590	            why = (
591	                "no specific regulated drug or drug class named — "
592	                "v30_clinical requires one for routed audits"
593	            )
594	        elif alien_count > 1:
595	            why = (
596	                f"query contains {alien_count} unrecognized content "
597	                f"tokens beyond what the v30_clinical catalog covers; "
598	                f"operator should clarify or trim non-clinical content"
599	            )
600	        else:
601	            why = (
602	                "drug named but query shape did not match a known "
603	                "exemplar; operator should reframe or confirm scope"
604	            )
605	        return RoutingResult(
606	            verdict=RoutingVerdict.OPERATOR_REVIEW,
607	            template_id=top.template_id,
608	            confidence=top.score,
609	            candidates=candidates,
610	            rationale=(
611	                f"Query partially matches '{top.template_id}' "
612	                f"(score {top.score:.2f} in "
613	                f"[{config.floor_review:.2f}, {config.floor_high:.2f})): "
614	                f"{why}. Operator must confirm scope before audit launches."
615	            ),
616	        )
617	
618	    return RoutingResult(
619	        verdict=RoutingVerdict.UNSUPPORTED,
620	        template_id=None,
621	        confidence=top.score,
622	        candidates=candidates,
623	        rationale=(
624	            f"Query does not match any supported audit template "
625	            f"(top candidate '{top.template_id}' scored {top.score:.2f}, "
626	            f"below floor_review {config.floor_review:.2f}). "
627	            f"See the scope page for supported question shapes."
628	        ),
629	    )

1	"""Retrieval-active workspace memory (M-21 — Phase C).
2	
3	Per FINAL_PLAN Phase C deliverable #4:
4	  Retrieval-active workspace memory (10-18 eng days, Phase C per
5	  Codex split):
6	    - User-visible, attributable, removable
7	    - Retrieved priors LABELED in Evidence Inspector view 1 as
8	      "memory-derived"
9	    - Workspace boundaries strict; no cross-customer leakage
10	    - Freshness/staleness rules
11	
12	Scope of v1:
13	  - Persist a workspace's memory entries (claim_text + source_url
14	    + tier + created_at + workspace_id) in SQLite.
15	  - retrieve(workspace_id, question) returns the top-K freshest
16	    memory entries that match question keywords. Pure deterministic
17	    retrieval — no LLM in the loop, no network.
18	  - Cross-workspace isolation: every API call requires workspace_id;
19	    the store layer scopes ALL reads + writes to that single
20	    workspace. Cross-workspace bleed is the dominant Phase C
21	    failure mode.
22	  - Freshness: entries carry created_at; retrieve() respects an
23	    optional `max_age_days` cutoff.
24	  - Removable: delete_entry(workspace_id, entry_id) hard-deletes.
25	    No silent retention.
26	
27	Out of scope for v1 (intentionally — keep the LAW V "one
28	responsibility per module" surface narrow):
29	  - Embedding-based similarity. The keyword-overlap retriever in
30	    v1 is enough for the Inspector "memory-derived" label
31	    integration; we add embedding retrieval in a v2 milestone
32	    when the Phase D memory-quality work begins.
33	  - Cross-workspace propagation. Per the FINAL_PLAN, global-system
34	    memory is "quarantined from audit lane by default" — the
35	    workspace-memory module does not write to that surface, and
36	    the audit lane never reads from it.
37	  - Bulk import. Memory entries land via append_entry() one at a
38	    time, called by the V30 runner when an entry meets a memory-
39	    worthiness gate (deferred to runner integration).
40	
41	LAW VII compliance: this module imports only from stdlib. No
42	reach-back into runner / generator. The endpoint that wires the
43	store into FastAPI is added in inspector_router.py with the auth
44	dependency on require_workspace_*.
45	"""

138	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
139	# Tokenization — DELEGATES to the M-10 template_classifier surface
140	# so both modules see identical vocabulary semantics (Unicode-
141	# hyphen normalization, Roman numeral collapse, compact drug-class
142	# split, stopword filter). Codex M-21 v1 review surfaced that the
143	# original ad-hoc tokenizer here drifted: "GLP-1" did not retrieve
144	# "GLP1 receptor agonist", "phase 3" missed "phase III", and
145	# "type 2 diabetes" only partially overlapped "type II diabetes".
146	# Reusing the M-10 path closes those drift gaps in one shot.

150	def _tokenize(text: str) -> set[str]:
151	    """Stopword-filtered token set using the M-10 tokenizer.
152	
153	    Returns a SET (order-insensitive) for Jaccard scoring. The
154	    M-10 functions handle Unicode hyphen normalization, Roman
155	    numerals (II/III/IV/VI..IX -> Arabic), compact drug-class
156	    forms (GLP1 -> [glp, 1]), and 1-character noise suppression.
157	    """
158	    from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.template_classifier import (
159	        _filter_stopwords, _tokenize_raw,
160	    )
161	    if not text:
162	        return set()
163	    return set(_filter_stopwords(_tokenize_raw(text)))

171	class WorkspaceMemoryStore:
172	    """SQLite-backed workspace memory.

174	    Per-call connections (matches WorkspaceStore + JobQueue +
175	    ReviewStore pattern). WAL journal for concurrent reads.
176	    Cross-workspace isolation: every method takes workspace_id
177	    and SQL filters on it.
178	    """
179	
180	    def __init__(self, db_path: Path) -> None:
181	        self._db_path = Path(db_path)
182	        self._db_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
183	        self._init_schema()
184	
185	    def _connect(self) -> sqlite3.Connection:
186	        conn = sqlite3.connect(
187	            self._db_path, isolation_level=None, timeout=30.0,
188	        )
189	        conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
190	        conn.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL")
191	        conn.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON")
192	        return conn
193	
194	    def _init_schema(self) -> None:
195	        with self._connect() as conn:
196	            conn.executescript(_SCHEMA)
197	
198	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
199	    # Append
200	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
201	
202	    def append_entry(
203	        self,
204	        *,
205	        workspace_id: str,
206	        claim_text: str,
207	        source_url: str,
208	        source_tier: str,
209	        source_evidence_id: str | None = None,
210	    ) -> MemoryEntry:
211	        """Persist one memory entry. Cross-workspace isolation: the
212	        entry is bound to workspace_id; reads from another workspace
213	        cannot see it."""
214	        if not workspace_id.strip():
215	            raise WorkspaceMemoryStateError(
216	                "workspace_id must be non-empty"
217	            )
218	        claim = claim_text.strip()
219	        if not claim:
220	            raise WorkspaceMemoryStateError(
221	                "claim_text must be non-empty after stripping; "
222	                "memory entries must carry concrete prose"
223	            )
224	        url = source_url.strip()
225	        if not url:
226	            raise WorkspaceMemoryStateError(
227	                "source_url must be non-empty; the Inspector cannot "
228	                "show a memory-derived label without an attribution "
229	                "link to the original source"
230	            )
231	        tier = (source_tier or "").strip()
232	        if not tier:
233	            raise WorkspaceMemoryStateError(
234	                "source_tier must be non-empty; tier is part of the "
235	                "memory-derived attribution surface"
236	            )
237	        entry_id = f"mem_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}"
238	        now = time.time()
239	        with self._connect() as conn:
240	            conn.execute(
241	                "INSERT INTO workspace_memory (entry_id, workspace_id, "
242	                "claim_text, source_url, source_tier, "
243	                "source_evidence_id, created_at) "
244	                "VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)",
245	                (
246	                    entry_id, workspace_id.strip(), claim, url, tier,
247	                    (source_evidence_id or None), now,
248	                ),
249	            )
250	        return MemoryEntry(
251	            entry_id=entry_id, workspace_id=workspace_id.strip(),
252	            claim_text=claim, source_url=url, source_tier=tier,
253	            source_evidence_id=(source_evidence_id or None),
254	            created_at=now, last_used_at=None,
255	        )
256	
257	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
258	    # Read
259	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
260	
261	    def get_entry(
262	        self, *, workspace_id: str, entry_id: str,
263	    ) -> MemoryEntry | None:
264	        """Single-entry read. Returns None if the entry doesn't
265	        exist OR if it belongs to a different workspace (no
266	        existence leak)."""
267	        with self._connect() as conn:
268	            row = conn.execute(
269	                "SELECT * FROM workspace_memory "
270	                "WHERE entry_id = ? AND workspace_id = ?",
271	                (entry_id, workspace_id),
272	            ).fetchone()
273	        if row is None:
274	            return None
275	        return _row_to_entry(row)
276	
277	    def list_entries(
278	        self,
279	        *,
280	        workspace_id: str,
281	        max_age_days: float | None = None,
282	    ) -> list[MemoryEntry]:
283	        """List entries for a workspace, newest first.
284	
285	        `max_age_days` (FINAL_PLAN: freshness/staleness rules) is
286	        applied at SQL level: entries older than the cutoff are
287	        excluded. Pass None to disable the cutoff.
288	        """
289	        if max_age_days is not None and max_age_days < 0:
290	            raise WorkspaceMemoryStateError(
291	                "max_age_days must be >= 0 if provided"
292	            )
293	        params: list[Any] = [workspace_id]
294	        sql = (
295	            "SELECT * FROM workspace_memory WHERE workspace_id = ? "
296	        )
297	        if max_age_days is not None:
298	            cutoff = time.time() - max_age_days * 86400.0
299	            sql += "AND created_at >= ? "
300	            params.append(cutoff)
301	        sql += "ORDER BY created_at DESC"
302	        with self._connect() as conn:
303	            rows = conn.execute(sql, params).fetchall()
304	        return [_row_to_entry(r) for r in rows]
305	
306	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
307	    # Retrieve (the active piece — for the runner to call before
308	    # generating)
309	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
310	
311	    def retrieve(
312	        self,
313	        *,
314	        workspace_id: str,
315	        query: str,
316	        top_k: int = 10,
317	        max_age_days: float | None = None,
318	    ) -> list[tuple[MemoryEntry, float]]:
319	        """Return up to `top_k` entries matching `query` keywords,
320	        scored by Jaccard overlap on stopword-filtered tokens.
321	        Each result is (entry, score) with score in [0, 1].
322	
323	        Side effect: bumps `last_used_at` on each retrieved entry
324	        so freshness ranking can favor recently-useful memory.
325	        Per LAW II we surface this as deterministic SQL rather
326	        than a "magic refresh" — caller can audit the timestamps.
327	
328	        Cross-workspace isolation: only entries from workspace_id
329	        are even considered. The retrieval cannot leak across
330	        workspaces because the SQL WHERE filters on it.
331	        """
332	        if not workspace_id.strip():
333	            raise WorkspaceMemoryStateError(
334	                "workspace_id must be non-empty"
335	            )
336	        if top_k < 1:
337	            raise WorkspaceMemoryStateError("top_k must be >= 1")
338	        q_tokens = _tokenize(query)
339	        if not q_tokens:
340	            return []
341	        entries = self.list_entries(
342	            workspace_id=workspace_id, max_age_days=max_age_days,
343	        )
344	        scored: list[tuple[MemoryEntry, float]] = []
345	        for e in entries:
346	            e_tokens = _tokenize(e.claim_text)
347	            if not e_tokens:
348	                continue
349	            inter = q_tokens & e_tokens
350	            if not inter:
351	                continue
352	            union = q_tokens | e_tokens
353	            jaccard = len(inter) / len(union)
354	            scored.append((e, jaccard))
355	        scored.sort(key=lambda t: (-t[1], -t[0].created_at))
356	        top = scored[:top_k]
357	
358	        if top:
359	            now = time.time()
360	            ids = [e.entry_id for (e, _s) in top]
361	            placeholders = ",".join("?" * len(ids))
362	            with self._connect() as conn:
363	                conn.execute(
364	                    f"UPDATE workspace_memory SET last_used_at = ? "
365	                    f"WHERE workspace_id = ? AND entry_id IN ({placeholders})",
366	                    [now, workspace_id, *ids],
367	                )
368	
369	        return top
370	
371	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
372	    # Delete
373	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
374	
375	    def delete_entry(
376	        self, *, workspace_id: str, entry_id: str,
377	    ) -> bool:
378	        """Hard-delete one entry. Returns True if a row was deleted,
379	        False otherwise (entry didn't exist or belonged to another
380	        workspace).
381	
382	        Per FINAL_PLAN, memory must be user-removable. Hard delete
383	        (no soft-delete tombstones) so customers can guarantee a
384	        deletion request actually purged the underlying data.
385	
386	        Codex M-21 v1 review fix: WAL journal mode keeps deleted
387	        bytes in `*-wal` until the next checkpoint, so a forensic
388	        scan of the file would still find them. After every
389	        delete we issue `PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)` which
390	        flushes the WAL into the main DB and shrinks the WAL file
391	        to zero bytes. This is the strongest purge guarantee
392	        SQLite gives us in WAL mode.
393	        """
394	        with self._connect() as conn:
395	            cur = conn.execute(
396	                "DELETE FROM workspace_memory "
397	                "WHERE entry_id = ? AND workspace_id = ?",
398	                (entry_id, workspace_id),
399	            )
400	            deleted = cur.rowcount > 0
401	            if deleted:
402	                self._wal_truncate(conn)
403	        return deleted
404	
405	    def delete_all_for_workspace(self, *, workspace_id: str) -> int:
406	        """Bulk-delete every entry for a workspace.
407	
408	        Used by workspace deletion / audit-bundle "wipe" requests.
409	        Returns the number of rows deleted. Codex M-21 v1 fix:
410	        WAL is truncated after the delete (see delete_entry).
411	        """
412	        with self._connect() as conn:
413	            cur = conn.execute(
414	                "DELETE FROM workspace_memory WHERE workspace_id = ?",
415	                (workspace_id,),
416	            )
417	            deleted = cur.rowcount
418	            if deleted > 0:
419	                self._wal_truncate(conn)
420	        return deleted
421	
422	    @staticmethod
423	    def _wal_truncate(conn: sqlite3.Connection) -> None:
424	        """Flush WAL into the main DB and shrink the WAL file to
425	        zero bytes. Best-effort: if `wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)`
426	        is unavailable, fall back to PASSIVE which still flushes
427	        but doesn't shrink the file."""
428	        try:
429	            conn.execute("PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)")
430	        except sqlite3.OperationalError:
431	            try:
432	                conn.execute("PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(PASSIVE)")
433	            except sqlite3.OperationalError:
434	                pass
435	
436	
437	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
438	# Row → object converter
439	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
440	
441	
442	def _row_to_entry(row: sqlite3.Row) -> MemoryEntry:
443	    return MemoryEntry(
444	        entry_id=row["entry_id"],
445	        workspace_id=row["workspace_id"],
446	        claim_text=row["claim_text"],
447	        source_url=row["source_url"],
448	        source_tier=row["source_tier"],
449	        source_evidence_id=row["source_evidence_id"],
450	        created_at=float(row["created_at"]),
451	        last_used_at=(
452	            float(row["last_used_at"]) if row["last_used_at"] is not None
453	            else None
454	        ),
455	    )

1	"""Workspace + upload data model (M-11 — Phase B foundation).
2	
3	Per FINAL_PLAN.md: "Bounded upload + workspace data model — 10-50
4	docs/workspace, persistent". This module ships the SQLite-backed
5	foundation: workspaces, uploads, parser status state machine,
6	bounded enforcement, soft-delete with audit trail.
7	
8	OUT OF SCOPE for Phase B:
9	  - Filter modes (uploaded-only / web-only / blended) — M-12
10	  - Retrieval over uploads — M-12
11	  - ACL / RBAC beyond workspace_id scoping — Phase C
12	  - Real PDF/sheet/slide parsers — Phase C
13	
14	State machine for upload.parser_status:
15	  pending  → parsing            (worker picks it up)
16	  parsing  → parsed | failed    (parse completes)
17	  any      → deleted_at SET     (soft-delete; audit trail)
18	
19	Bounded enforcement:
20	  Each workspace has a `max_docs` (env-overridable default).
21	  upload_file() raises BoundedError if the workspace already holds
22	  `max_docs` non-deleted uploads. Fails LOUD per LAW II — no
23	  silent truncation.
24	
25	Mirrors M-8 JobQueue patterns:
26	  - SQLite WAL mode, per-call connections.
27	  - Atomic state transitions via UPDATE..WHERE..AND status='X'.
28	  - Foreign keys enabled.
29	"""

165	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspaces (
166	    workspace_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
167	    name TEXT NOT NULL,
168	    max_docs INTEGER NOT NULL,
169	    created_at REAL NOT NULL,
170	    org_id TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'org_default'
171	);

174	
175	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS uploads (
176	    upload_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
177	    workspace_id TEXT NOT NULL,
178	    filename TEXT NOT NULL,
179	    content_type TEXT,
180	    size_bytes INTEGER NOT NULL,
181	    storage_path TEXT NOT NULL,
182	    parser_status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
183	    parser_error TEXT,
184	    created_at REAL NOT NULL,
185	    parsed_at REAL,
186	    deleted_at REAL,
187	    FOREIGN KEY (workspace_id) REFERENCES workspaces(workspace_id)
188	);

190	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_uploads_workspace
191	    ON uploads(workspace_id, deleted_at);
192	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_uploads_parser_status
193	    ON uploads(parser_status, deleted_at);
194	
195	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS upload_chunks (
196	    chunk_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
197	    upload_id TEXT NOT NULL,
198	    seq INTEGER NOT NULL,
199	    text TEXT NOT NULL,
200	    provenance_json TEXT NOT NULL,
201	    FOREIGN KEY (upload_id) REFERENCES uploads(upload_id)
202	);

204	CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_upload_chunks_upload
205	    ON upload_chunks(upload_id, seq);

209	class WorkspaceStore:
210	    """SQLite-backed workspace + upload registry.
211	
212	    Per-call connections (matches JobQueue pattern). WAL mode for
213	    concurrent reads. Foreign keys enabled.
214	    """
215	
216	    def __init__(self, db_path: Path) -> None:
217	        self._db_path = Path(db_path)
218	        self._db_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
219	        self._init_schema()
220	
221	    def _connect(self) -> sqlite3.Connection:
222	        conn = sqlite3.connect(self._db_path, isolation_level=None, timeout=30.0)
223	        conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
224	        conn.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL")
225	        conn.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON")
226	        return conn
227	
228	    def _init_schema(self) -> None:
229	        with self._connect() as conn:
230	            conn.executescript(_SCHEMA)
231	            # Codex M-15b review fix: migration for pre-M-15b
232	            # databases that lack the org_id column. CREATE TABLE
233	            # IF NOT EXISTS is a no-op when the table already
234	            # exists, so an old DB never picks up new columns.
235	            # Add the column lazily here.
236	            self._migrate_workspaces_org_id(conn)
237	            self._migrate_uploads_indexes(conn)
238	
239	    @staticmethod
240	    def _migrate_workspaces_org_id(conn: sqlite3.Connection) -> None:
241	        cols = [
242	            r[1] for r in conn.execute(
243	                "PRAGMA table_info(workspaces)"
244	            ).fetchall()
245	        ]
246	        if "org_id" not in cols:
247	            conn.execute(
248	                "ALTER TABLE workspaces ADD COLUMN org_id TEXT NOT NULL "
249	                "DEFAULT 'org_default'"
250	            )
251	            conn.execute(
252	                "CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_workspaces_org "
253	                "ON workspaces(org_id)"
254	            )
255	
256	    @staticmethod
257	    def _migrate_uploads_indexes(conn: sqlite3.Connection) -> None:
258	        # Future-proofing hook; no-op for now.
259	        pass

265	    def create_workspace(
266	        self,
267	        name: str,
268	        max_docs: int | None = None,
269	        org_id: str = "org_default",
270	    ) -> Workspace:
271	        """Codex M-15b review fix: `org_id` is now a parameter.
272	        Defaults to `org_default` so the (small number of)
273	        Phase A-vintage callers without an org tag still work.
274	        Phase C callers (M-15b retrofitted endpoints) must pass
275	        the authenticated caller's org_id."""
276	        if not name or not name.strip():
277	            raise WorkspaceStateError("workspace name must be non-empty")
278	        if max_docs is None:
279	            max_docs = _env_max_docs()
280	        if max_docs < 1:
281	            raise WorkspaceStateError(f"max_docs must be >= 1; got {max_docs}")
282	        if not org_id or not org_id.strip():
283	            raise WorkspaceStateError("org_id must be non-empty")
284	        ws_id = f"ws_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}"
285	        now = time.time()
286	        with self._connect() as conn:
287	            conn.execute(
288	                "INSERT INTO workspaces (workspace_id, name, max_docs, "
289	                "created_at, org_id) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)",
290	                (ws_id, name.strip(), max_docs, now, org_id.strip()),
291	            )
292	        return Workspace(
293	            workspace_id=ws_id, name=name.strip(),
294	            max_docs=max_docs, created_at=now, org_id=org_id.strip(),
295	        )

298	        """Codex M-15b review fix: list endpoint must scope to the

304	                "ORDER BY created_at DESC",

309	    def get_workspace(self, workspace_id: str) -> Workspace | None:
310	        with self._connect() as conn:
311	            row = conn.execute(
312	                "SELECT * FROM workspaces WHERE workspace_id = ?",
313	                (workspace_id,),
314	            ).fetchone()

316	
317	    def list_workspaces(self) -> list[Workspace]:
318	        with self._connect() as conn:
319	            rows = conn.execute(
320	                "SELECT * FROM workspaces ORDER BY created_at DESC"
321	            ).fetchall()
322	        return [_row_to_workspace(r) for r in rows]

324	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
325	    # Uploads
326	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
327	
328	    def upload_file(
329	        self,
330	        workspace_id: str,
331	        filename: str,
332	        content_type: str | None,
333	        size_bytes: int,
334	        storage_path: str,
335	    ) -> Upload:
336	        """Register a new upload. Atomically validates that the
337	        workspace exists AND has spare capacity. Raises BoundedError
338	        if at the cap; WorkspaceStateError if the workspace is
339	        unknown.
340	
341	        Codex M-11 review fix: enforcement is now transactional via
342	        BEGIN IMMEDIATE — without it, two concurrent uploads could
343	        both see (max_docs - 1) and oversubscribe the cap. The
344	        IMMEDIATE lock acquires SQLite's write lock at BEGIN time,
345	        so the second connection blocks until the first commits.
346	        """
347	        if not filename:
348	            raise WorkspaceStateError("filename must be non-empty")
349	        if size_bytes < 0:
350	            raise WorkspaceStateError(f"size_bytes must be >= 0; got {size_bytes}")
351	        upload_id = f"up_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}"
352	        now = time.time()
353	        with self._connect() as conn:
354	            conn.execute("BEGIN IMMEDIATE")
355	            try:
356	                ws_row = conn.execute(
357	                    "SELECT max_docs FROM workspaces WHERE workspace_id = ?",
358	                    (workspace_id,),
359	                ).fetchone()
360	                if ws_row is None:
361	                    conn.execute("ROLLBACK")
362	                    raise WorkspaceStateError(
363	                        f"unknown workspace: {workspace_id}"
364	                    )
365	                max_docs = ws_row["max_docs"]
366	                cur = conn.execute(
367	                    "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM uploads "
368	                    "WHERE workspace_id = ? AND deleted_at IS NULL",
369	                    (workspace_id,),
370	                )
371	                current = cur.fetchone()[0]
372	                if current >= max_docs:
373	                    conn.execute("ROLLBACK")
374	                    raise BoundedError(
375	                        f"workspace {workspace_id} at cap "
376	                        f"({current}/{max_docs}); delete an upload or "
377	                        f"raise PG_WORKSPACE_MAX_DOCS"
378	                    )
379	                conn.execute(
380	                    "INSERT INTO uploads (upload_id, workspace_id, filename, "
381	                    "content_type, size_bytes, storage_path, parser_status, "
382	                    "created_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, 'pending', ?)",
383	                    (upload_id, workspace_id, filename, content_type,
384	                     size_bytes, storage_path, now),
385	                )
386	                conn.execute("COMMIT")
387	            except (BoundedError, WorkspaceStateError):
388	                # Already rolled back above.
389	                raise
390	            except Exception:
391	                conn.execute("ROLLBACK")
392	                raise
393	        return Upload(
394	            upload_id=upload_id, workspace_id=workspace_id, filename=filename,
395	            content_type=content_type, size_bytes=size_bytes,
396	            storage_path=storage_path, parser_status="pending",
397	            parser_error=None, created_at=now, parsed_at=None, deleted_at=None,
398	        )
