1	"""I-cred-002 (Phase 2, L1 / §9.1) — adaptive LLM credibility skill (reliability × relevance).
2	
3	ONE generic, domain-agnostic credibility skill. Per research question it scores EACH candidate
4	source on RELIABILITY × RELEVANCE with a written, inspectable rationale, consuming POLARIS's
5	already-computed deterministic authority signals (``authority_score`` / ``source_class`` /
6	``corroboration_count`` / ``authority_confidence`` / ``signal_scores`` / ``junk_class`` /
7	``predatory_oa``) as its PRIORS. There are NO fixed domain rubrics: the detected ``domain`` is a
8	single HINT field the judge reasons over, never a branch that swaps rubrics — so this scales to any
9	field (operator directive 2026-06-08, plan §9.1).
10	
11	FAITHFULNESS POSTURE (binding):
12	  * ADVISORY ONLY. This never becomes a faithfulness gate — ``strict_verify``'s six per-sentence
13	    checks (``generator/provenance_generator.py``) remain the ONLY binding gate. A credibility
14	    weight/rationale is a side-output to disclose, never a reason to keep or drop a sentence.
15	  * DEFAULT-OFF byte-identical: ``PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_SKILL`` (no production caller is added here).
16	  * The LLM call is DEPENDENCY-INJECTED (``judge``); with no judge → no network, no client, no spend
17	    (mirrors ``retrieval/semantic_conflict_detector``). The production judge is wired by the caller,
18	    never constructed in this pure library.
19	  * NO row mutation, NO faithfulness-file import. Pure functions, snake_case, explicit imports.
20	  * LAW VI: every threshold is an env-overridable named constant (no magic numbers, no hardcoded
21	    model/endpoint).
22	
23	ANTI-FABRICATION (the LOW/thin guardrail): for a LOW-confidence or thin-signal source the judge's
24	reliability is capped at ``clamp01(authority_score) + PG_CREDIBILITY_MAX_UPLIFT`` — the model cannot
25	invent authority that the deterministic signals do not support. The judge MAY freely DOWN-rate any
26	source below its prior (a high-authority but irrelevant/weak source can score low); the prior is NOT
27	a lower bound. The deterministic priors-only judgment is the fallback ONLY on a judge error / no
28	judge, never a universal floor.
29	"""
30	from __future__ import annotations
31	
32	import math
33	import os
34	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
35	from typing import Any, Callable, Optional
36	
37	# ── flag (default OFF — matches supersession.py / claim_graph.py) ─────────────
38	_FLAG = "PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_SKILL"
39	_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"", "0", "false", "off", "no"})
40	
41	# ── env knobs (LAW VI: named, env-overridable, no magic numbers) ──────────────
42	_ENV_MAX_UPLIFT = "PG_CREDIBILITY_MAX_UPLIFT"
43	_ENV_SNIPPET_CHARS = "PG_CREDIBILITY_SNIPPET_CHARS"
44	_DEFAULT_MAX_UPLIFT = 0.15
45	_DEFAULT_SNIPPET_CHARS = 1200
46	# I-arch-002 (#1251): per-source judging concurrency. The judge is a SYNC LLM call (~24s at full
47	# reasoning); over hundreds of sources SEQUENTIALLY it froze the run. Default 12 parallel workers. 1
48	# forces the byte-identical sequential path (off-mode / single source / tests).
49	_ENV_JUDGE_CONCURRENCY = "PG_CREDIBILITY_JUDGE_CONCURRENCY"
50	_DEFAULT_JUDGE_CONCURRENCY = 12
51	# I-deepfix-001 (credibility-pass HANG backstop): a HARD wall on the pool-JOIN so a stalled / provider-
52	# pinned judge worker can NEVER freeze the run. Each worker already carries a ~300s per-call total-
53	# deadline, but the ``as_completed()`` join had NO outer bound — under a 429 / empty-provider-window storm
54	# every worker re-enters retry+backoff and the whole pass grinds past the sweep wall (the live faulthandler
55	# tonight: credibility_skill as_completed blocked, main asyncio loop parked). On the wall we STOP joining,
56	# fill every un-scored source with its deterministic priors (judge_error=True), and RETURN. Generous
57	# default so a HEALTHY pass (completes in minutes) NEVER trips it — it only bounds a true multi-hour stall;
58	# the run slate may lower it to coordinate with PG_CREDIBILITY_PASS_WALL_S. LAW VI: env-driven.
59	_ENV_JUDGE_POOL_WALL_S = "PG_CREDIBILITY_JUDGE_POOL_WALL_S"
60	_DEFAULT_JUDGE_POOL_WALL_S = 3600.0
61	# I-deepfix-001 (box2 SPEED fix): TIER-BAND HYBRID. A comma-list of source TIERS whose credibility the
62	# LLM judge SKIPS — those rows take their DETERMINISTIC authority prior instead (``_priors_only_judgment``:
63	# weight = authority_score, high for T1/T2, low for T6/T7, so the downstream demotion still fires). This
64	# reduces the per-run LLM REQUEST COUNT (the 429-relief lever) while keeping EVERY source credibility-
65	# weighted. It is faithfulness-neutral (the pass is ADVISORY) AND selection-neutral (breadth/selection
66	# rank on ``authority_score`` weight_mass, NOT this credibility — weight_mass.py plan §148). Default empty
67	# => hybrid OFF => every row is LLM-judged, byte-identical. LAW VI: env-driven.
68	_ENV_HYBRID_SKIP_TIERS = "PG_CREDIBILITY_JUDGE_HYBRID_TIERS"
69	# I-deepfix-001 (box2 SPEED fix): emit a progress line every N completed judge calls so a large pass can
70	# never again read as a dead run. 0/unset => no progress log (byte-identical). LAW VI: env-driven.
71	_ENV_PROGRESS_EVERY = "PG_CREDIBILITY_JUDGE_PROGRESS_EVERY"
72	
73	# The deterministic prior-signal names the judge may cite (anti-hallucination: a
74	# ``signals_cited`` entry that is not one of these AND present on the row is dropped).
75	_PRIOR_SIGNAL_KEYS = (
76	    "authority_score",
77	    "source_class",
78	    "corroboration_count",
79	    "authority_confidence",
80	    "signal_scores",
81	    "junk_class",
82	    "predatory_oa",
83	    "origin_cluster_id",  # Phase-4 independence signal (present once Phase 6 wires P4 -> P2)
84	)
85	
86	
87	def credibility_skill_enabled() -> bool:
88	    """True unless ``PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_SKILL`` is unset/falsey (default OFF => byte-identical).
89	
90	    Caller kill-switch: no production caller invokes this library while OFF, so the rendered report
91	    + manifest are unchanged. The pure functions below do NOT read the flag — they are total +
92	    offline-testable; the caller gates invocation.
93	    """
94	    return os.environ.get(_FLAG, "").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
95	
96	
97	def _float_env(name: str, default: float) -> float:
98	    try:
99	        return float(os.environ.get(name, "") or default)
100	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
101	        return default
102	
103	
104	def _int_env(name: str, default: int) -> int:
105	    try:
106	        return int(os.environ.get(name, "") or default)
107	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
108	        return default
109	
110	
111	def _clamp01(value: Any) -> float | None:
112	    """Clamp a numeric to [0, 1]. Returns ``None`` for non-numeric / NaN / inf (the caller treats
113	    that as a judge error — never a NaN weight, never a crash)."""
114	    try:
115	        x = float(value)
116	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
117	        return None
118	    if math.isnan(x) or math.isinf(x):
119	        return None
120	    return 0.0 if x < 0.0 else 1.0 if x > 1.0 else x
121	
122	
123	@dataclass
124	class CredibilityJudgment:
125	    """One source's advisory credibility judgment (a side-output, never a verdict input).
126	
127	    ``reliability_score`` is AFTER the anti-fabrication cap; ``relevance_score`` is the judged
128	    directness to the question (unknown => 1.0, multiplicative-neutral); ``credibility_weight`` is
129	    the FIXED product ``clamp01(reliability * relevance)``. ``signals_cited`` is the subset of the
130	    deterministic prior signals the judge reasoned from. ``judge_error`` is True iff the injected
131	    judge raised / returned malformed output for THIS source (isolated; priors-only fallback used).
132	    """
133	
134	    evidence_id: str
135	    reliability_score: float
136	    relevance_score: float
137	    credibility_weight: float
138	    rationale: str
139	    signals_cited: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
140	    query_need: str = ""
141	    judge_error: bool = False
142	
143	
144	def _present_signals(row: dict[str, Any]) -> list[str]:
145	    """The deterministic prior signals actually present (non-empty) on this row."""
146	    return [k for k in _PRIOR_SIGNAL_KEYS if row.get(k) not in (None, "", {}, [])]
147	
148	
149	def _row_snippet(row: dict[str, Any], max_chars: int) -> str:
150	    text = str(row.get("direct_quote") or row.get("statement") or row.get("text") or "")
151	    return text[:max_chars]
152	
153	
154	def _build_judge_payload(
155	    research_question: str, row: dict[str, Any], domain: str | None
156	) -> dict[str, Any]:
157	    """Pure: assemble the judge payload for ONE source. Does NOT mutate ``row``.
158	
159	    Carries source identity + bounded descriptors (title / url / snippet — so RELEVANCE is actually
160	    judgeable) + the deterministic authority priors + the ``domain_hint`` (a single string field,
161	    NOT a branch / rubric table). The judge sees ONLY this payload.
162	    """
163	    snippet_chars = _int_env(_ENV_SNIPPET_CHARS, _DEFAULT_SNIPPET_CHARS)
164	    return {
165	        "research_question": research_question,
166	        "evidence_id": str(row.get("evidence_id", "")),
167	        "title": str(row.get("title", "") or ""),
168	        "url": str(row.get("source_url", "") or row.get("url", "") or ""),
169	        "snippet": _row_snippet(row, snippet_chars),
170	        "authority_score": row.get("authority_score"),
171	        "source_class": row.get("source_class"),
172	        "corroboration_count": row.get("corroboration_count"),
173	        "authority_confidence": row.get("authority_confidence"),
174	        "signal_scores": dict(row.get("signal_scores") or {}),
175	        "junk_class": row.get("junk_class", ""),
176	        "predatory_oa": row.get("predatory_oa", False),
177	        "origin_cluster_id": row.get("origin_cluster_id"),  # Phase-4 independence signal (None pre-wiring)
178	        "domain_hint": domain or "",
179	    }
180	
181	
182	def _is_low_or_thin(row: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
183	    """A source is LOW/thin (subject to the anti-fabrication cap) when its authority_confidence is
184	    LOW, or it has no authority_score, or it has no signal_scores."""
185	    if str(row.get("authority_confidence") or "").strip().upper() == "LOW":
186	        return True
187	    if row.get("authority_score") is None:
188	        return True
189	    if not (row.get("signal_scores") or {}):
190	        return True
191	    return False
192	
193	
194	def _hybrid_skip_tiers() -> frozenset[str]:
195	    """I-deepfix-001 (box2 SPEED fix): the UPPER-cased set of source tiers the LLM judge SKIPS (those
196	    rows take their deterministic authority prior). Empty (unset) => hybrid OFF => every row LLM-judged,
197	    byte-identical. LAW VI: env-driven (``PG_CREDIBILITY_JUDGE_HYBRID_TIERS``, e.g. ``T1,T2,T6,T7``)."""
198	    raw = os.environ.get(_ENV_HYBRID_SKIP_TIERS, "").strip()
199	    if not raw:
200	        return frozenset()
201	    return frozenset(t.strip().upper() for t in raw.split(",") if t.strip())
202	
203	
204	def _row_tier(row: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
205	    """The source tier label on a row (``tier`` then ``source_tier``), UPPER-cased; '' when unknown.
206	    An UNKNOWN/absent tier is NEVER in a skip-set of concrete tiers, so it always routes to the LLM
207	    judge — the safe direction (ambiguous rows are judged, never skipped)."""
208	    return str(row.get("tier") or row.get("source_tier") or "").strip().upper()
209	
210	
211	def _priors_only_judgment(row: dict[str, Any]) -> CredibilityJudgment:
212	    """The deterministic fallback (no judge / judge error): reliability from the prior, relevance
213	    neutral (1.0). Never fabricates relevance it cannot judge."""
214	    auth = _clamp01(row.get("authority_score"))
215	    reliability = auth if auth is not None else 0.0
216	    return CredibilityJudgment(
217	        evidence_id=str(row.get("evidence_id", "")),
218	        reliability_score=reliability,
219	        relevance_score=1.0,
220	        credibility_weight=reliability,  # == clamp01(reliability * 1.0)
221	        rationale="no judge wired — deterministic priors only",
222	        signals_cited=_present_signals(row),
223	        query_need="",
224	        judge_error=False,
225	    )
226	
227	
228	def _apply_judge(
229	    research_question: str,
230	    row: dict[str, Any],
231	    domain: str | None,
232	    judge: Callable[[str, dict], dict],
233	) -> CredibilityJudgment:
234	    """Run the injected judge for ONE source; fall back to priors on ANY error / malformed output
235	    (isolated to this row — recall-first, fail-loud-but-bounded)."""
236	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import BudgetExceededError
237	    payload = _build_judge_payload(research_question, row, domain)
238	    try:
239	        raw = judge(research_question, payload)
240	    except BudgetExceededError:
241	        # Codex #012a iter-3 P1: a budget-cap breach MUST escape this per-row handler to the sweep's
242	        # budget-abort path — NOT be masked as a per-row judge_error/priors-only fallback.
243	        raise
244	    except Exception:
245	        fallback = _priors_only_judgment(row)
246	        fallback.judge_error = True
247	        return fallback
248	    if not isinstance(raw, dict):
249	        fallback = _priors_only_judgment(row)
250	        fallback.judge_error = True
251	        return fallback
252	
253	    reliability = _clamp01(raw.get("reliability_score"))
254	    if reliability is None:  # missing / NaN / inf reliability => judge error for this row
255	        fallback = _priors_only_judgment(row)
256	        fallback.judge_error = True
257	        return fallback
258	    relevance = _clamp01(raw.get("relevance_score"))
259	    if relevance is None:  # unknown / malformed relevance => multiplicative-neutral
260	        relevance = 1.0
261	
262	    # Anti-fabrication cap: a LOW/thin source's reliability cannot exceed prior + max_uplift.
263	    if _is_low_or_thin(row):
264	        auth = _clamp01(row.get("authority_score")) or 0.0
265	        cap = _clamp01(auth + _float_env(_ENV_MAX_UPLIFT, _DEFAULT_MAX_UPLIFT))
266	        if cap is not None:
267	            reliability = min(reliability, cap)
268	
269	    present = set(_present_signals(row))
270	    cited = [s for s in (raw.get("signals_cited") or []) if s in present]
271	    weight = _clamp01(reliability * relevance)
272	    return CredibilityJudgment(
273	        evidence_id=str(row.get("evidence_id", "")),
274	        reliability_score=reliability,
275	        relevance_score=relevance,
276	        credibility_weight=weight if weight is not None else 0.0,
277	        rationale=str(raw.get("rationale", "")),
278	        signals_cited=cited,
279	        query_need=str(raw.get("query_need", "")),
280	        judge_error=False,
281	    )
282	
283	
284	def score_source_credibility(
285	    research_question: str,
286	    rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
287	    *,
288	    domain: str | None = None,
289	    judge: Optional[Callable[[str, dict], dict]] = None,
290	    pool_wall_s: float | None = None,
291	) -> list[CredibilityJudgment]:
292	    """Score each source on reliability × relevance — ADVISORY ONLY, pure, no row mutation.
293	
294	    With ``judge=None`` returns one priors-only judgment per row (no network, total + offline). With
295	    an injected ``judge(research_question, payload) -> dict`` runs it PER SOURCE so a judge error is
296	    isolated to one row. ``domain`` is forwarded only as the ``domain_hint`` payload field — there is
297	    no domain-keyed branch or rubric table (operator: no fixed rubrics).
298	
299	    I-arch-002 (#1251): the judge is a SYNCHRONOUS LLM call; over hundreds of sources run SEQUENTIALLY on
300	    the asyncio MainThread it FROZE the pipeline (py-spy: ssl.recv). With ``PG_CREDIBILITY_JUDGE_CONCURRENCY``
301	    > 1 the per-source calls run CONCURRENTLY, mirroring the proven 4-role parallel cost pattern
302	    (``roles/sweep_integration.py``): each source runs in its OWN ``contextvars.copy_context()`` snapshot
303	    taken on THIS thread (so it inherits the parent run_id + Path-B capture sink), ``reset_run_cost()``s its
304	    copy, and returns ``current_run_cost()`` as a per-source delta the parent re-adds to the SINGLE run
305	    counter and re-checks the cap with DURING-compute enforcement (overspend bounded to ~workers in flight).
306	    Order is preserved (downstream ``zip(rows, judgments)`` requires it). A worker ``BudgetExceededError`` /
307	    exception PROPAGATES (fail closed; no silent drop). The caller (``multi_section_generator`` 5383) runs
308	    this whole pass under ``asyncio.to_thread`` so the blocking pool join never sits on the event loop.
309	
310	    ``pool_wall_s`` (I-deepfix-001 BANK-BEFORE-WALL, #1264 follow-up): an OPTIONAL caller-supplied
311	    upper bound on the pool-join wall — the EFFECTIVE wall is ``min(PG_CREDIBILITY_JUDGE_POOL_WALL_S,
312	    pool_wall_s)``. ``credibility_pass._run_chain`` threads its remaining deadline budget here so this
313	    phase can NEVER outlive the caller's pass wall (the drb_72 box1 defect: the env wall defaulted to
314	    3600s while the outer pass wall was force-pinned at 3000s, so the graceful partial-bank below was
315	    UNREACHABLE and the whole analysis was discarded — the corroboration layer vanished from
316	    report.md). ``None`` (every legacy caller) => env wall only, byte-identical. Behavior at the wall
317	    is UNCHANGED: drain the finished futures, fill the rest with deterministic priors, never discard.
318	    """
319	    rows = rows or []
320	    if judge is None:
321	        return [_priors_only_judgment(row) for row in rows]
322	
323	    # I-deepfix-001 (box2 SPEED fix) — TIER-BAND HYBRID: route CLEAR-tier rows to their deterministic
324	    # authority prior (no LLM call) and LLM-judge only the AMBIGUOUS tiers, cutting the per-run request
325	    # count (the 429-relief lever). Every source still ends with a real credibility weight; selection /
326	    # breadth rank on authority weight_mass (NOT this credibility), so this is selection-neutral +
327	    # faithfulness-neutral (advisory pass). Empty skip-set => hybrid OFF => every row LLM-judged
328	    # (byte-identical). Recurses with an empty skip-set on the ambiguous subset so the pooled path below
329	    # is reused verbatim (order preserved).
330	    _skip_tiers = _hybrid_skip_tiers()
331	    if _skip_tiers:
332	        out: list[Optional[CredibilityJudgment]] = [None] * len(rows)
333	        ambiguous_rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
334	        ambiguous_idx: list[int] = []
335	        for _i, _row in enumerate(rows):
336	            _tier = _row_tier(_row)
337	            if _tier in _skip_tiers:
338	                _pj = _priors_only_judgment(_row)
339	                _pj.rationale = (
340	                    f"tier-clear ({_tier or 'n/a'}): deterministic authority prior; LLM relevance-"
341	                    "judging reserved for ambiguous-tier sources (hybrid request-count reduction)"
342	                )
343	                out[_i] = _pj
344	            else:
345	                ambiguous_rows.append(_row)
346	                ambiguous_idx.append(_i)
347	        if ambiguous_rows:
348	            judged = _judge_rows_pooled(
349	                research_question, ambiguous_rows, domain, judge, pool_wall_s=pool_wall_s,
350	            )
351	            for _k, _j in enumerate(judged):
352	                out[ambiguous_idx[_k]] = _j
353	        import logging as _logging  # noqa: PLC0415
354	        _logging.getLogger(__name__).info(
355	            "[credibility] hybrid tiering: %d/%d LLM-judged (ambiguous), %d clear-tier via "
356	            "deterministic authority prior (skip_tiers=%s) — request-count reduction, every source "
357	            "still weighted; selection ranks on authority weight_mass (unchanged)",
358	            len(ambiguous_rows), len(rows), len(rows) - len(ambiguous_rows), sorted(_skip_tiers),
359	        )
360	        return [j for j in out if j is not None]
361	
362	    return _judge_rows_pooled(research_question, rows, domain, judge, pool_wall_s=pool_wall_s)
363	
364	
365	def _judge_rows_pooled(
366	    research_question: str,
367	    rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
368	    domain: str | None,
369	    judge: Callable[[str, dict], dict],
370	    *,
371	    pool_wall_s: float | None = None,
372	) -> list[CredibilityJudgment]:
373	    """Run the injected judge over ``rows`` (bounded-parallel, order-preserved) — the pooled body
374	    shared by the plain path and the hybrid ambiguous-subset path.
375	
376	    Verdict-identical to the pre-refactor inline pool: only WHICH rows are handed to it differs (all
377	    rows on the plain path; the ambiguous subset on the hybrid path). I-deepfix-001 (box2 SPEED fix)
378	    adds an OPTIONAL progress log (``PG_CREDIBILITY_JUDGE_PROGRESS_EVERY``, default 0 => silent) so a
379	    large pass can never again read as a dead run."""
380	    n = len(rows)
381	    try:
382	        workers = max(1, int(
383	            os.environ.get(_ENV_JUDGE_CONCURRENCY, _DEFAULT_JUDGE_CONCURRENCY) or _DEFAULT_JUDGE_CONCURRENCY
384	        ))
385	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
386	        workers = _DEFAULT_JUDGE_CONCURRENCY
387	    # SEQUENTIAL fast path (1 worker or <=1 row): byte-identical to the pre-#1251 listcomp.
388	    if workers == 1 or n <= 1:
389	        return [_apply_judge(research_question, row, domain, judge) for row in rows]
390	
391	    import concurrent.futures
392	    import contextvars
393	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import (
394	        reset_run_cost, current_run_cost, _add_run_cost, check_run_budget,
395	        BudgetExceededError,
396	    )
397	
398	    def _score_one(
399	        idx: int, row: dict[str, Any], ctx: contextvars.Context
400	    ) -> tuple[int, CredibilityJudgment, float]:
401	        def _run() -> tuple[CredibilityJudgment, float]:
402	            reset_run_cost()  # isolate THIS source's spend in the copied context (parent re-adds a clean delta)
403	            judgment = _apply_judge(research_question, row, domain, judge)
404	            return judgment, current_run_cost()
405	        result, delta = ctx.run(_run)
406	        return idx, result, delta
407	
408	    # I-deepfix-001 (HANG backstop): the pool JOIN wall — see the _DEFAULT_JUDGE_POOL_WALL_S note above.
409	    pool_wall = _float_env(_ENV_JUDGE_POOL_WALL_S, _DEFAULT_JUDGE_POOL_WALL_S)
410	    # I-deepfix-001 BANK-BEFORE-WALL: the caller's remaining deadline budget CAPS the env wall (min of
411	    # the two) so this phase can never outlive the outer credibility-pass wall. None => env-only.
412	    if pool_wall_s is not None:
413	        pool_wall = min(pool_wall, max(1.0, float(pool_wall_s)))
414	    # I-deepfix-001 (box2 SPEED fix): progress cadence (0 => silent, byte-identical).
415	    try:
416	        _progress_every = max(0, int(os.environ.get(_ENV_PROGRESS_EVERY, "0") or "0"))
417	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
418	        _progress_every = 0
419	    _completed = 0
420	
421	    results: list[Optional[CredibilityJudgment]] = [None] * n
422	    pool = concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=workers)
423	    timed_out = False
424	    try:
425	        futures = [
426	            pool.submit(_score_one, i, row, contextvars.copy_context())
427	            for i, row in enumerate(rows)
428	        ]
429	        try:
430	            # HARD wall on the join: a stalled worker can never freeze the run. as_completed raises
431	            # concurrent.futures.TimeoutError when pool_wall elapses before every worker joins.
432	            for future in concurrent.futures.as_completed(futures, timeout=pool_wall):
433	                idx, judgment, delta = future.result()  # re-raises BudgetExceededError / worker exc (fail closed)
434	                results[idx] = judgment
435	                _add_run_cost(delta)            # thread the per-source spend into the single run counter
436	                check_run_budget(0)             # raises BudgetExceededError -> bounded overspend (~workers in flight)
437	                _completed += 1
438	                if _progress_every and (_completed % _progress_every == 0):
439	                    import logging as _logging  # noqa: PLC0415
440	                    _logging.getLogger(__name__).info(
441	                        "[credibility] judge progress: %d/%d sources scored (workers=%d)",
442	                        _completed, n, workers,
443	                    )
444	        except concurrent.futures.TimeoutError:
445	            # The wall fired before every worker joined. Drain any future that DID finish (so a real
446	            # verdict or a real BudgetExceededError is never lost); the rest are filled with priors below.
447	            timed_out = True
448	            for future, idx in ((f, i) for i, f in enumerate(futures)):
449	                if results[idx] is not None or not future.done():
450	                    continue
451	                try:
452	                    _d_idx, judgment, delta = future.result()
453	                except BudgetExceededError:
454	                    raise  # a real cap breach MUST still abort the sweep, even on the wall path
455	                except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a genuinely-failed worker degrades to priors below
456	                    continue
457	                results[idx] = judgment
458	                _add_run_cost(delta)
459	                # Codex diff-gate iter1 P0: the wall-drain path MUST enforce the aggregate budget just
460	                # like the healthy join (which does _add_run_cost THEN check_run_budget(0)). Without this
461	                # a burst of drained futures could push the run past PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN without ever
462	                # raising BudgetExceededError. It is OUTSIDE the per-future try above, so the breach
463	                # propagates to the outer BaseException handler -> non-blocking shutdown + re-raise
464	                # (fail-closed cap abort), never swallowed by the drain's `except Exception: continue`.
465	                check_run_budget(0)
466	    except BaseException:
467	        pool.shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True)
468	        raise
469	    else:
470	        # Timed-out: don't block on the still-running stalled workers (wait=False) and cancel the queued
471	        # ones; the healthy path joins normally (wait=True).
472	        pool.shutdown(wait=not timed_out, cancel_futures=timed_out)
473	
474	    if timed_out:
475	        stalled = [i for i, j in enumerate(results) if j is None]
476	        import logging as _logging  # noqa: PLC0415
477	        _logging.getLogger(__name__).warning(
478	            "[credibility] judge pool wall-deadline (PG_CREDIBILITY_JUDGE_POOL_WALL_S=%.0fs) fired with "
479	            "%d/%d source(s) still unscored; filling them with DETERMINISTIC priors (judge_error=True -> "
480	            "credibility_pass LABELS them credibility_unscored) and returning — the run NEVER hangs. The "
481	            "advisory pass degrades; strict_verify / NLI / 4-role D8 / span-grounding are untouched.",
482	            pool_wall, len(stalled), n,
483	        )
484	        for i in stalled:
485	            fallback = _priors_only_judgment(rows[i])
486	            fallback.judge_error = True  # fail-closed: LABEL as credibility_unscored, never a silent drop
487	            results[i] = fallback
488	
489	    for i, judgment in enumerate(results):
490	        if judgment is None:
491	            raise RuntimeError(
492	                f"score_source_credibility: source index {i} produced no judgment from the compute pool "
493	                f"(fail-closed — a dropped future must never reduce silently)."
494	            )
495	    return [j for j in results if j is not None]
496

1	"""Single cached loader for all ``config/authority/*`` versioned data files.
2	
3	Phase 0a (GH #983). LAW VI: every threshold/weight/map/pattern the authority
4	model uses is loaded from versioned DATA here, never inlined in a ``.py`` file
5	(the S4-grep test enforces zero host/suffix literals in code).
6	
7	LAW II — fail-loud: a missing / empty / unparseable data file raises
8	``AuthorityDataError``; the loader NEVER returns a silent default.
9	"""
10	from __future__ import annotations
11	
12	import functools
13	import os
14	from pathlib import Path
15	from typing import Any
16	
17	import yaml
18	
19	
20	class AuthorityDataError(RuntimeError):
21	    """Raised when a versioned authority data file is missing/empty/unparseable."""
22	
23	
24	# Resolve config/authority/ relative to the repo root (this file lives at
25	# src/polaris_graph/authority/data_loader.py -> repo root is parents[3]).
26	_DEFAULT_DATA_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3] / "config" / "authority"
27	
28	
29	def _data_dir() -> Path:
30	    """Data dir, overridable via env for tests (LAW VI — no hardcode)."""
31	    override = os.getenv("PG_AUTHORITY_DATA_DIR", "")
32	    return Path(override) if override else _DEFAULT_DATA_DIR
33	
34	
35	def _require_file(name: str) -> Path:
36	    path = _data_dir() / name
37	    if not path.exists():
38	        raise AuthorityDataError(
39	            f"authority data file missing: {path} "
40	            f"(set PG_AUTHORITY_DATA_DIR or ship config/authority/{name})"
41	        )
42	    if path.stat().st_size == 0:
43	        raise AuthorityDataError(f"authority data file is empty: {path}")
44	    return path
45	
46	
47	def _load_yaml(name: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
48	    path = _require_file(name)
49	    try:
50	        data = yaml.safe_load(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
51	    except yaml.YAMLError as exc:
52	        raise AuthorityDataError(f"authority data file unparseable: {path}: {exc}") from exc
53	    if not isinstance(data, dict) or not data:
54	        raise AuthorityDataError(f"authority data file empty/invalid mapping: {path}")
55	    return data
56	
57	
58	def _load_suffix_list(name: str) -> tuple[str, ...]:
59	    path = _require_file(name)
60	    suffixes: list[str] = []
61	    for raw in path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines():
62	        line = raw.strip()
63	        if not line or line.startswith("#"):
64	            continue
65	        suffixes.append(line.lower())
66	    if not suffixes:
67	        raise AuthorityDataError(f"authority suffix list has no entries: {path}")
68	    # de-dup preserving order
69	    seen: set[str] = set()
70	    ordered: list[str] = []
71	    for s in suffixes:
72	        if s not in seen:
73	            seen.add(s)
74	            ordered.append(s)
75	    return tuple(ordered)
76	
77	
78	@functools.lru_cache(maxsize=1)
79	def _bundle_for_dir(data_dir: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
80	    """Load + cache the whole bundle for a specific data dir."""
81	    version_path = Path(data_dir) / "VERSION"
82	    if not version_path.exists() or version_path.stat().st_size == 0:
83	        raise AuthorityDataError(f"authority VERSION missing/empty: {version_path}")
84	    version = version_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").strip()
85	    if not version:
86	        raise AuthorityDataError(f"authority VERSION blank: {version_path}")
87	    return {
88	        "version": version,
89	        "scholarly_weights": _load_yaml("scholarly_weights.yaml"),
90	        "ror_type_class_map": _load_yaml("ror_type_class_map.yaml"),
91	        "junk_patterns": _load_yaml("junk_patterns.yaml"),
92	        "recency_profile": _load_yaml("recency_profile.yaml"),
93	        "blend_weights": _load_yaml("blend_weights.yaml"),
94	        "clinical_view": _load_yaml("clinical_view.yaml"),
95	        "psl_gov_suffixes": _load_suffix_list("psl_gov_suffixes.txt"),
96	    }
97	
98	
99	def load_authority_data() -> dict[str, Any]:
100	    """Return the cached, validated authority data bundle (fail-loud)."""
101	    return _bundle_for_dir(str(_data_dir()))
102	
103	
104	def clear_cache() -> None:
105	    """Drop the cached bundle (tests that swap PG_AUTHORITY_DATA_DIR call this)."""
106	    _bundle_for_dir.cache_clear()
107

1	"""Signal B — institutional authority (the region generalizer).
2	
3	Phase 0a (GH #983). Data-driven (LAW VI). ZERO host names in code.
4	
5	Order of evidence (load-bearing, per brief §4 Signal B):
6	  1. ROR institution-type (OpenAlex authorships[].institutions[].ror) — LOAD-BEARING.
7	  2. PSL gov-style suffix on the host — FAST PRE-FILTER ONLY (a DNS boundary
8	     list, NOT a trust signal; misses canada.ca / bundesbank.de / rbi.org.in).
9	  3. Issuer self-description backstop — schema.org GovernmentOrganization /
10	     dc.publisher official-issuer pattern in the fetched structural content.
11	
12	The ROR-type -> source_class map + the score weights + the PSL gov subset all
13	live in config/authority/{ror_type_class_map.yaml,psl_gov_suffixes.txt}.
14	"""
15	from __future__ import annotations
16	
17	import re
18	from dataclasses import dataclass
19	
20	from src.polaris_graph.authority.source_class import (
21	    AuthorityConfidence,
22	    AuthoritySignals,
23	    SourceClass,
24	)
25	
26	# Structural self-description regexes (NOT host names — schema.org / meta-tag
27	# shapes). These are the ONLY literals here and they are markup tokens, not
28	# hosts/suffixes/platforms, so they do not trip the S4 zero-host grep.
29	_SELF_DESC_OFFICIAL_RE = re.compile(
30	    r'"@type"\s*:\s*"GovernmentOrganization"'
31	    r'|<meta[^>]+name=["\']dc\.publisher["\'][^>]*'
32	    r'(?:ministry|government|regulatory|administration|authority)',
33	    re.IGNORECASE,
34	)
35	
36	
37	@dataclass
38	class SignalBResult:
39	    score: float
40	    source_class: SourceClass
41	    confidence: AuthorityConfidence
42	    reasons: list[str]
43	    fired: bool
44	
45	
46	def host_matches_gov_suffix(host: str, gov_suffixes: tuple[str, ...]) -> bool:
47	    """True if host equals or ends with '.'+suffix for any gov suffix."""
48	    if not host:
49	        return False
50	    host = host.lower().rstrip(".")
51	    for suffix in gov_suffixes:
52	        if host == suffix or host.endswith("." + suffix):
53	            return True
54	    return False
55	
56	
57	def _class_from_ror_type(
58	    inst_type: str, ror_map: dict
59	) -> tuple[SourceClass, AuthorityConfidence] | None:
60	    key = (inst_type or "").strip().lower()
61	    if not key:
62	        return None
63	    entry = ror_map["type_to_class"].get(key)
64	    if not entry:
65	        return None
66	    return SourceClass(entry["source_class"]), AuthorityConfidence(entry["confidence"])
67	
68	
69	def compute_signal_b(
70	    host: str,
71	    signals: AuthoritySignals,
72	    structural_content: str,
73	    ror_map: dict,
74	    gov_suffixes: tuple[str, ...],
75	) -> SignalBResult:
76	    """Compute institutional authority sub-score + a candidate source_class."""
77	    reasons: list[str] = []
78	    weights = ror_map["score_weights"]
79	
80	    sub_scores: list[float] = []
81	    candidate_class: SourceClass | None = None
82	    candidate_conf: AuthorityConfidence | None = None
83	
84	    # 1) ROR institution-type (load-bearing).
85	    ror_resolved = _class_from_ror_type(signals.institution_type, ror_map)
86	    if signals.ror_id and ror_resolved is not None:
87	        candidate_class, candidate_conf = ror_resolved
88	        sub_scores.append(weights["ror_resolved"])
89	        reasons.append(
90	            f"ROR institution type {signals.institution_type!r} "
91	            f"-> {candidate_class.value} (load-bearing)"
92	        )
93	
94	    # 2) PSL gov-suffix pre-filter.
95	    if host_matches_gov_suffix(host, gov_suffixes):
96	        sub_scores.append(weights["psl_gov_suffix"])
97	        reasons.append("host carries a gov-style public suffix (PSL pre-filter)")
98	        if candidate_class is None:
99	            candidate_class = SourceClass.PRIMARY_OFFICIAL
100	            candidate_conf = AuthorityConfidence.MEDIUM
101	
102	    # 3) Issuer self-description backstop.
103	    if structural_content and _SELF_DESC_OFFICIAL_RE.search(structural_content):
104	        sub_scores.append(weights["issuer_self_desc"])
105	        reasons.append("issuer self-describes as official (schema.org / dc.publisher)")
106	        if candidate_class is None:
107	            candidate_class = SourceClass.PRIMARY_OFFICIAL
108	            candidate_conf = AuthorityConfidence.LOW
109	
110	    fired = bool(sub_scores)
111	    if not fired:
112	        return SignalBResult(
113	            score=ror_map["neutral_score"],
114	            source_class=SourceClass.UNKNOWN,
115	            confidence=AuthorityConfidence.LOW,
116	            reasons=["no institutional signal (no ROR, no gov suffix, no self-desc)"],
117	            fired=False,
118	        )
119	
120	    return SignalBResult(
121	        score=max(sub_scores),
122	        source_class=candidate_class or SourceClass.UNKNOWN,
123	        confidence=candidate_conf or AuthorityConfidence.LOW,
124	        reasons=reasons,
125	        fired=True,
126	    )
127
